specs.title: Curator web panel (scoped) + curator Telegram notifications

spec.summary:
Extend the existing /panel admin dashboard to a third role — curator — with a STRICTLY self-scoped view (only profiles where curator_id = session.uid), plus Telegram push notifications to a student's curator on lifecycle events. The work has two halves that share one new helper (curatorChatIdForStudent).

PANEL (scoped):
- Widen AdminRole to include 'curator' in session.ts (the HMAC token format is unchanged; only the union type and the layout/nav gating change). The bot /panel command and panel/auth/telegram already block non-admins — both must be relaxed to allow curator and mint a curator-scoped session.
- The panel uses service-role everywhere and bypasses Postgres RLS by design (stats-data.ts comment: "Данные — через service-role (обход RLS, это сервер админа)"). Therefore curator scoping is NOT enforceable via Postgres RLS in the panel path — it MUST be enforced in application code: every panel data path must filter by curator_id === session.uid when session.role === 'curator'. This is the single biggest correctness risk. resolveIsOwner stays admin-only (a curator is never owner).
- Curator sees: Обзор (their group only), Ученики (their group only), per-student detail (only if that student's curator_id === self, else 404). Curator must NOT see: Кураторы, Города, Заявки, GenerateReport, role/transfer/delete/attach actions.

NOTIFICATIONS:
- Recipient = student's curator_id -> telegram_chat_id (new helper curatorChatIdForStudent). Five events: (1) approved/registered as student, (2) first entry to platform, (3) course started, (4) day closed, (5) block completed. Events 1-3 have natural single hooks; events 4-5 have NO single event (passed_blocks_all() is recomputed from the daily model), so they are detected by recompute-and-compare inside the day-closing submit routes, guarded by an idempotency table to avoid duplicate pushes.

Effort is M-L: panel scoping is moderate (touch ~6 files, all guard-style edits) but the day-closed / block-completed notification detection requires a new idempotency table + a shared recompute helper wired into 4-5 submit routes, which is the bulk of the risk and testing.
spec.dataModel:
All changes are additive, IF NOT EXISTS, RLS-on. No column drops.

=== Migration 1: supabase/migrations/20260621100000_v3_curator_notify_state.sql ===
Idempotency ledger so day-closed / block-completed / first-entry pushes fire exactly once per (student, event-key). Without this, recompute-on-every-submit would re-notify on every cross/prayer mark of the same day.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS curator_notify_state (
  id           bigint GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
  student_id   uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  curator_id   uuid REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  event_type   text NOT NULL,            -- 'registered'|'first_entry'|'course_started'|'day_closed'|'block_completed'
  event_key    text NOT NULL,            -- '' for once-ever; 'YYYY-MM-DD' for day_closed; 'block:<n>' for block_completed
  created_at   timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE (student_id, event_type, event_key)
);
ALTER TABLE curator_notify_state ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
-- No anon/auth policies: only service_role (server) touches it. RLS-on with zero policies = deny-all to clients, which is the goal.
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_cns_student ON curator_notify_state(student_id);
GRANT SELECT, INSERT ON curator_notify_state TO service_role;

Notes:
- event_key uses '' (empty string) not NULL so the UNIQUE constraint actually dedupes once-ever events (NULLs are distinct in Postgres UNIQUE).
- day_closed event_key = the closed-day date string the day-close detection returns.
- block_completed event_key = 'block:'||N where N is the newly-passed block number.

=== No new columns on profiles ===
curator_id, telegram_chat_id, role, is_protected already exist and are sufficient. We deliberately do NOT add a "first_entry_at" column; first-entry is recorded in curator_notify_state (event_type='first_entry', key='').

=== RPC reused, not changed ===
passed_blocks_all() (per-user blocks_passed) and user_closed_days(p_user_id) already exist and are the source of truth for block-completed / day-closed detection. No new RPC required, but optionally add a single-user wrapper for efficiency (see API section).

=== notifications_log ===
Already exists (20260521200100_v3_curator_notifications_log.sql). Reuse it: every curator push also inserts a row { user_id: student_id, channel:'telegram', type:'curator_<event>', status:'sent'|'failed' } for audit, mirroring the daily-reminder cron pattern.
spec.apiRoutes:
Legend: [CHANGE] existing file, [ADD] new file. Auth column = how the route authorizes.

--- PANEL AUTH (allow curator) ---

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/lib/admin/session.ts
  - `export type AdminRole = 'admin' | 'super_admin' | 'curator'`
  - signSession / signLoginToken / verifySession unchanged (token format identical; only the union widens). Add a helper `export function isCurator(s: AdminSession){return s.role==='curator'}` for call-site clarity.

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts  (Auth: telegram_chat_id -> profiles.role)
  - In the `/panel` | `/site` | `/dashboard` branch (currently lines ~1219-1245): change the gate from `['admin','super_admin'].includes(prof.role)` to also allow `'curator'`. Mint `signLoginToken({uid, role: prof.role as AdminRole, name})`. Curator gets the same enter link; scope is enforced downstream by role in the session.

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/auth/telegram/route.ts  (Auth: Telegram Login Widget HMAC)
  - Relax the 403 check (line 39) to allow role==='curator'. Sign the session with the real role.

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/enter/route.ts  (Auth: short-lived signed token)
  - No logic change needed; it already re-signs with sess.role, which now may be 'curator'. Verify only.

--- PANEL DATA (scope by curator_id) ---
Pattern for every panel data path: read session; if role==='curator', add `.eq('curator_id', session.uid)` to the student query AND drop the cross-curator "curators map"/global aggregates.

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/students/route.ts  (Auth: getPanelSessionFromReq)
  - After loading session: `const scopeCuratorId = session.role==='curator' ? session.uid : null`.
  - Filter rows: `.filter(p => p.role==='student' && (scopeCuratorId ? p.curator_id===scopeCuratorId : true) && (isOwner || !p.hidden_from_tracking))`. Curator is never owner, so hidden students stay hidden.
  - For curators, return `curators: []` (no curator dropdown — they cannot reassign).

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/student/[id]/route.ts  (Auth: getPanelSessionFromReq)
  - After fetching the profile: if `session.role==='curator' && profile.curator_id !== session.uid` -> return 404 `{ok:false,error:'Ученик не найден'}` (same shape as not-found, so a curator can't probe existence of others' students).

[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/stats/stats-data.ts + stats/route.ts  (Auth: getPanelSessionFromReq)
  - getPanelStats currently takes only isOwner. Add a second arg `scopeCuratorId?: string`. When set, `visibleStudents` filters additionally by `curator_id===scopeCuratorId`; byCity/byCountry/progress/streaks/stuck all derive from that scoped `students` set already, so they become group-local automatically. totals.curators/admins should be hidden for curator scope (set to 0 or omit in UI).
  - stats/route.ts and panel/(dash)/page.tsx pass `session.role==='curator' ? session.uid : undefined`.

--- CURATOR-FORBIDDEN ROUTES (must 403 for curator) ---
These already gate "session exists" but treat all sessions as admin. Add an explicit `if (session.role==='curator') return 403` at the top of each:
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/role/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/transfer/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/delete/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/attach/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/add/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/actions/access-request/route.ts
[CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/panel/cities/route.ts
  (Defense in depth: even though the nav hides these, a curator with a valid cookie could POST directly.)

--- NOTIFICATION HELPERS (new) ---

[ADD] apps/web/src/lib/telegram/curator-recipients.ts
  - `export async function curatorChatIdForStudent(supabase, studentId): Promise<number|null>`:
    select curator_id from profiles where id=studentId; if null -> null; select telegram_chat_id from profiles where id=curator_id AND role IN ('curator','admin','super_admin'); return finite chat id or null.
  - `export async function notifyCurator(supabase, studentId, text): Promise<void>`: resolve chat id; if null, no-op (do NOT fall back to admins — curator notifications are private). sendTelegramMessage(...).catch(swallow). Insert notifications_log row.

[ADD] apps/web/src/lib/telegram/curator-events.ts  (the recompute + dedupe engine)
  - `export async function fireDayAndBlockEvents(supabase, studentId)`: 
    1) call passed_blocks_all() (or single-user RPC) -> current blocks_passed; call user_closed_days(studentId) -> closed days per block.
    2) Determine "today closed?" via the same logic block-status uses; the canonical closed-day date is computed there. For day_closed, the event_key is that date.
    3) Try INSERT into curator_notify_state (student_id, curator_id, 'day_closed', <date>) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; if a row was inserted (rowCount=1) -> notifyCurator(day-closed text).
    4) For block_completed: if blocks_passed increased vs the max 'block:'<n> already recorded, INSERT 'block_completed','block:'||blocks_passed; on fresh insert -> notifyCurator(block text). Idempotent via UNIQUE.
  - This function is called fire-and-forget (not awaited blocking the user response) at the END of each day-closing submit route.

--- WHERE EACH EVENT FIRES ---

(1) registered/approved as student -> [CHANGE] webhook/route.ts handleCallbackQuery 'approve_student' branch (after createTelegramProfile, ~line 256): notifyCurator only if the just-created profile has a curator_id (from /attach assigned_curator_id). Most approvals have no curator yet, so usually no-op; covered for the attach-first flow. event_type='registered', key=''.

(2) first entry to platform -> [CHANGE] apps/web/src/lib/telegram/resolve-user.ts (or the first /m/* call that resolves a student). On successful resolveUserId for a student, fireFirstEntry(supabase, studentId): INSERT curator_notify_state('first_entry','') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; on fresh row -> notifyCurator("вошёл в платформу впервые"). Single source so any /m route triggers it once.

(3) course started -> [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/api/miniapp/onboarding/route.ts. It already notifies admins (lines 86-100). Add a sibling call: notifyCurator(student.curator_id, "начал курс"), guarded by curator_notify_state('course_started',''). Only when course_started_at was just set (the .is('course_started_at', null) update path) to avoid re-fires on re-onboarding.

(4) day closed + (5) block completed -> [CHANGE] each day-closing submit route, append `void fireDayAndBlockEvents(supabase, userId)` before the JSON response:
   - apps/web/src/app/api/m/cross-photo/upload/route.ts (or cross-photo/[blockId])
   - apps/web/src/app/api/m/prayer/[blockId]/route.ts
   - apps/web/src/app/api/m/recitation/upload/route.ts
   - apps/web/src/app/api/m/locations/upload/route.ts
   - apps/web/src/app/api/m/quiz/submit/route.ts
   fireDayAndBlockEvents internally dedupes, so calling from all five is safe and only the submit that actually completes the 5/5 day (or 7th day -> block) pushes.

--- OPTIONAL RPC for efficiency ---
[ADD migration] passed_blocks_one(p_user_id uuid) returning int — avoids scanning all students on every submit. If skipped, reuse passed_blocks_all() and find the row (acceptable for current scale).
spec.rls:
Two distinct enforcement layers — they are NOT the same here, and conflating them is the trap:

1) PANEL = application-level scoping, NOT Postgres RLS.
   The panel runs entirely on service-role (createServiceSupabase), which bypasses RLS by design — stats-data.ts literally documents "Данные — через service-role (обход RLS)". So adding/strengthening Postgres RLS policies does NOTHING for the panel. Curator scoping MUST be enforced in TypeScript at every panel data path by `.eq('curator_id', session.uid)` (lists/stats) and `profile.curator_id===session.uid else 404` (detail). The session role is the trust anchor; it is HMAC-signed (cannot be tampered without ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET). Checklist of guarded paths: panel/students, panel/student/[id], panel/stats(stats-data), and hard-403 on actions/{role,transfer,delete,attach,add,access-request} and cities.

2) BOT = already application-scoped, mostly correct.
   The webhook resolves role by telegram_chat_id and `loadStudentsSummary`/`formatStudentDetail` already filter `curator_id === requester.id` for curators and reject "не твой ученик" (lines 328, 476). Those paths are SAFE as-is. The only bot change for this feature is allowing curators through the /panel command — which only mints a (downstream-scoped) web session, leaking nothing by itself.

3) curator_notify_state table: RLS enabled with ZERO policies = deny-all to anon/authenticated; only service_role (server) can read/insert. No client ever queries it.

4) notifications_log: unchanged; written server-side via service-role as today.

5) Privacy of pushes: notifyCurator resolves the recipient from the STUDENT's curator_id only — it never broadcasts to all curators and never falls back to admins (unlike admin-recipients). A student with curator_id=NULL simply produces no push. This guarantees a curator can never receive events about a student who is not theirs, in the bot OR the panel.

6) hidden_from_tracking: a curator is never the owner (resolveIsOwner stays admin-only), so hidden students remain invisible to curators in panel lists/stats and in /student detail — consistent with existing isOwner gating. If an owner ever needs a student hidden from their own curator too, that already holds because hidden filtering is independent of scope.
spec.ui: All panel UI reuses existing panel.css classes; no new CSS files (per existing convention). React auto-escape only (no innerHTML). [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/(dash)/PanelNav.tsx - Accept `role` (already passed). Build LINKS conditionally: curator -> [Обзор, Ученики] only (drop Заявки, Кураторы, Города). admin/super_admin -> full list as today. - Footer role label: add `role==='curator' ? 'Куратор'` to the existing ternary (currently only super_admin/admin). - Sidebar subtitle "Панель администратора" -> conditional "Панель куратора" for curators. [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/(dash)/layout.tsx - Currently allows any session (the only gate is `if(!session) redirect`). KEEP allowing curator (no change to the gate) but only fetch pendingCount for admins: `const pendingCount = session.role==='curator' ? 0 : await countPendingRequests(...)` (curators don't see Заявки anyway). Pass session.role to PanelNav (already does via session.role). [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/(dash)/page.tsx (Обзор) - Pass curator scope into getPanelStats. Hide the "Кураторов"/"Администраторов" stat tiles for curators (they only manage students). Hide GenerateReport for curators (`{session.role!=='curator' && }`). Title becomes "Моя группа" for curators. byCity/byCountry blocks are still fine (will show only their group's cities) but can be collapsed to a single "Город" tile if the group is one city — optional polish. [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/(dash)/students/page.tsx + StudentsClient.tsx + StudentRowActions.tsx - StudentsClient calls POST /api/panel/students which is now scoped server-side, so the list is automatically the curator's group. - StudentRowActions: hide the curator-reassign dropdown and delete/role controls when the viewer is a curator. Pass a `readOnly`/`role` prop down (the client already knows the role only via server; simplest: include `viewerRole` in the /api/panel/students response and branch in the client). Curators get a read-only roster (name, city, blocks, closed days, link to detail). [CHANGE] apps/web/src/app/panel/(dash)/students/[id]/page.tsx + StudentDetailClient.tsx - Detail GET now 404s for non-owned students. Client shows read-only progress (already mostly read-only); hide any admin-only action buttons for curators. [NO new pages] — curators reuse Обзор + Ученики + detail. Routes /panel/curators, /panel/cities, /panel/requests are simply not linked for curators; for defense, each of those Server Component pages should add `const s=await getPanelSession(); if(s?.role==='curator') redirect('/panel')` at top (3 one-line edits) so a curator typing the URL gets bounced. Telegram push copy (Russian, HTML, escapeHtml on names): - registered: "👤 Твой ученик {name} ({handle}) одобрен и привязан к тебе." - first_entry: "✨ {name} впервые открыл приложение." - course_started: "🎓 {name} начал курс КРЕСТ." - day_closed: "✅ {name} закрыл день ({closed}/7 в блоке {block})." - block_completed: "🏆 {name} сдал блок {n} из 10!" All sent via sendTelegramMessage(curatorChatId, text, { withMiniAppButton:true }).
implementationOrder: 1. Migration 20260621100000_v3_curator_notify_state.sql (table + RLS deny-all + grants). Apply, regenerate packages/supabase types, run tsc --noEmit (per memory: regen types after every migration or Vercel build silently breaks).
2. session.ts: widen AdminRole to include 'curator' + add isCurator helper. tsc --noEmit to surface every call site that switches on role.
3. Panel auth in: webhook /panel branch, panel/auth/telegram, panel/enter — allow curator to obtain a session. Manually test a curator can reach /panel.

4. Panel scoping (read paths): panel/students, panel/student/[id], stats-data.ts+stats/route.ts+page.tsx — add curator_id filter / 404. Verify a curator sees ONLY their group and 404s on others' students.

5. Panel hard-deny (write paths + forbidden pages): 403 in actions/{role,transfer,delete,attach,add,access-request} and cities route; redirect-guard in curators/cities/requests Server Component pages.

6. UI: PanelNav conditional links + role label; layout pendingCount guard; page.tsx hide admin tiles/report; StudentsClient/StudentRowActions/StudentDetailClient read-only for curators (add viewerRole to /api/panel/students response).

7. Notification helpers: curator-recipients.ts (curatorChatIdForStudent, notifyCurator) + curator-events.ts (fireFirstEntry, fireCourseStarted wrapper, fireDayAndBlockEvents with curator_notify_state dedupe).
8. Wire single-hook events: webhook approve_student (registered), miniapp/onboarding (course_started), resolve-user/first /m hit (first_entry).

9. Wire recompute events: append fireDayAndBlockEvents to cross-photo, prayer, recitation, locations, quiz/submit routes (fire-and-forget). Confirm via curator_notify_state that the 5/5 day and 7th-day-block push exactly once.
10. End-to-end test with a real curator+student pair on a test account; verify no cross-curator leakage in panel or bot; run npm --workspace=@krest/web run lint + build before handing off.
spec.risks:
RISK 1 (highest) — service-role bypasses RLS in the panel. If ANY panel data path forgets the curator_id filter, a curator sees the whole platform. Mitigation: centralize the scope decision (`const scopeCuratorId = session.role==='curator' ? session.uid : null`) and pass it explicitly into stats-data; add the 404 guard in student/[id]; hard-403 all action routes. Recommend a qa-reviewer RLS-style pass over every file under api/panel after step 5.

RISK 2 — "block completed" has no event. passed_blocks_all() is recomputed from the daily model (confirmed: migration comment "без block_passed_at"). There is no INSERT that means "block N passed". We therefore recompute on every day-closing submit and dedupe via curator_notify_state UNIQUE(student_id,'block_completed','block:'||n). Edge: a tester with can_skip_block_lock / test_daily_accel can close 7 days in one calendar day (prayer route stamps virtual 2000-01-01 dates) — block_completed still fires once because dedupe is by block number, not date. day_closed for accel users uses virtual dates, which is acceptable (still one push per virtual closed day).

RISK 3 — duplicate / spammy day_closed. cross+prayer+recitation+locations+quiz can all complete the same day; calling fireDayAndBlockEvents from all five means up to 5 concurrent recomputes racing the same INSERT. The UNIQUE constraint + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING makes it safe (only one wins), but use INSERT...ON CONFLICT and check rowCount, never SELECT-then-INSERT (would race). Fire-and-forget (`void`) so a Telegram hiccup never fails the student's submit.

RISK 4 — day-close date boundary. block-status/[blockId] uses todayUTC() while reminders use Bali date (cities.timezone exists but is unused). For day_closed event_key, reuse the SAME UTC convention block-status uses so "closed day" detection is consistent with what the student sees in the app; do not invent a Bali boundary here or the push and the UI checkmark could disagree. (Timezone unification is out of scope for this feature.)

RISK 5 — curator with no telegram_chat_id, or student with curator_id=NULL: notifyCurator no-ops (no admin fallback). Correct by design but means newly-registered students (no curator yet) generate no pushes until /attach — acceptable and documented.

RISK 6 — re-onboarding. onboarding route can run twice; course_started must only push when course_started_at transitions from NULL. Gate the notifyCurator on the same `.is('course_started_at', null)` path (check affected row), backed by curator_notify_state dedupe.

RISK 7 — token role tampering. Mitigated: session is HMAC-signed with ADMIN_SESSION_SECRET; a curator cannot forge role:'admin'. Just ensure no code trusts a client-supplied role over session.role.

RISK 8 — type regen. Per memory feedback_regen_types_after_migration: after migration 1, regenerate packages/supabase types and run tsc, or Vercel build passes locally but prod serves stale.

critique.verdict: The spec is directionally sound on the single biggest risk it names — panel runs on service-role and bypasses RLS, so curator scoping MUST be application-level — and that instinct is correct and verified. The forbidden-action routes today genuinely have NO role check beyond "session exists" (confirmed: actions/{role,transfer,delete,attach,add,access-request} all only call getPanelSessionFromReq and treat every session as admin), so the hard-403 plan is necessary, not paranoid. HMAC session signing, resolveIsOwner staying admin-only, and the deny-all RLS for the new idempotency table are all correct. HOWEVER the spec is built on a STALE mental model of the day-close domain and gets several concrete code references wrong. It must NOT be implemented as written. The "closed day = cross+prayer+recitation(audio)+mestopisaniya(video_note)+quiz" definition the prompt/spec uses does not exist anywhere in the current schema; the authoritative latest migration defines closed day = 3 sources (cross + prayer + audio-recitation) with locations and quiz REMOVED from the daily gate. The notifications_log reuse plan references the wrong migration and a column set the live table does not have. These are correctness gaps that would cause wrong/duplicate/never-firing pushes and a build break. Fix the corrections below before coding.
corrections: DAY-CLOSE MODEL IS WRONG (highest-impact). The spec repeatedly says a closed day = cross+prayer+recitation(audio)+mestopisaniya(video_note)+quiz (5 things), and RISK 2/RISK 4 lean on that. The ACTUAL current model (latest migration 20260620150000_v3_fix_locations_vs_peresказ.sql, which supersedes 20260620160000 mestopisaniya_daily despite the earlier timestamp ordering note — verify which actually ran last) defines: user_closed_days/closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all HAVING count(DISTINCT src) = 3, where the 3 sources are cross (student_block_daily_cross.submitted_date), prayer (student_block_daily_prayer.prayed_date), and audio-recitation (student_block_recitations medium='audio' passed). Quiz and locations are NOT part of the daily closed-day count: locations is a SEPARATE one-time block condition via locations_complete(), and quiz was explicitly dropped (20260620130000_v3_drop_quiz_from_gate.sql). The model churned 5→4→3 across migrations on 2026-06-20 — three different definitions exist in history. fireDayAndBlockEvents MUST derive 'day closed today' and 'block completed' SOLELY from the same RPCs the app uses (user_closed_days / passed_blocks_all), never re-implement the 5-task logic in TS, or the push will disagree with the UI checkmark.

BLOCK-STATUS ROUTE ALREADY DISAGREES WITH THE RPCs — do not copy its logic. apps/web/src/app/api/m/block-status/[blockId]/route.ts checks 4 today-flags (cross, prayer, pereskaz=audio, mestopisaniya=video_note-locations) using todayUTC() and direct table queries, NOT the 3-source RPC gate. So 'today.cross && today.prayer && today.pereskaz && today.mestopisaniya' in that route is NOT the same condition as user_closed_days incrementing. The spec says (apiRoutes, curator-events step 2) 'Determine today closed? via the same logic block-status uses; the canonical closed-day date is computed there' — that is incorrect and would mis-fire. The canonical closed-day signal is user_closed_days returning an incremented count, and the canonical date is the submission date. Detect via: snapshot closed-days count before, recompute after, and if it increased, fire with event_key = today's date (per RISK 4 keep it the UTC ::date boundary the RPCs use via (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date, since recitations/locations key off effective_date COALESCE UTC).

NOTIFICATIONS_LOG SCHEMA MISMATCH — the spec's audit-row plan will break. The spec (dataModel) says reuse 20260521200100_v3_curator_notifications_log.sql and insert { user_id, channel:'telegram', type, status }. But that migration defines notifications_log with columns curator_id NOT NULL, student_id NOT NULL, block_id, notification_type (CHECK IN a fixed enum: new_submission/silence_1day/silence_3days/block_exam_ready/exam_passed), read_at — NO user_id/channel/type/status. There are TWO conflicting CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications_log migrations (the OTHER is 20260426120002 with user_id/channel/type/status), so only the first-applied one exists and the second silently no-ops. The live cron (app/api/cron/daily-reminder/route.ts) inserts { user_id, channel, type, status } successfully → the 20260426 (user_id/channel) shape is the one that actually exists in prod. Therefore: (a) curator pushes that try to insert notification_type='curator_first_entry' etc. would VIOLATE the CHECK if the curator-version table existed, and (b) the spec cites the wrong migration. Decide explicitly: either log curator pushes with the live {user_id, channel:'telegram', type:'curator_<event>', status} shape, or add the new types to the CHECK via migration. Do NOT assume curator_id/student_id columns exist.

MIGRATION TIMESTAMP COLLISION RISK. The new migration is named 20260621100000_v3_curator_notify_state.sql but an existing migration is 20260621210000_v3_daily_reminder_20.sql (same date, later time). 100000 < 210000 so ordering is fine, but confirm no already-applied migration shares the 20260621100000 prefix and that supabase migration history won't reject an out-of-order insert if 210000 is already applied remotely (a migration timestamped earlier than the latest-applied can be rejected by `supabase db push`). Use a timestamp strictly greater than the latest applied migration (i.e. > 20260621210000), e.g. 20260622xxxxxx.

EVENT (3) course_started HOOK IS IN THE WRONG FILE/SHAPE. Spec says edit apps/web/src/app/api/miniapp/onboarding/route.ts and 'notifyCurator(student.curator_id, ...) only when course_started_at was just set via the .is(course_started_at,null) update path'. Reality (verified): that route sets course_started_at in a SEPARATE update chained .is('course_started_at', null) whose result is NOT captured — there is no affected-row count checked, and the admin notify block re-reads the profile unconditionally and fires every onboarding completion for role='student' (re-onboarding included). To fire course_started exactly once you must capture whether the .is(...) update actually changed a row (add .select('id') and check length) — the current code cannot tell first-time from re-onboarding for the notify. The curator_notify_state dedupe will save you, but the spec's claim that the existing .is() path already gates it is false.

EVENT (1) registered HOOK — verify the curator is knowable at approve time. Spec says fire in webhook approve_student branch 'if the just-created profile has a curator_id (from /attach assigned_curator_id)'. Verified: the approve_student branch calls createTelegramProfile({chatId,username,firstName,lastName,role}) with NO curator_id argument, and the access_requests update doesn't pass one. So at approval the profile.curator_id is essentially always NULL and event (1) will be a near-permanent no-op (the spec even admits 'usually no-op'). If the product wants a 'registered+attached' push, the real hook is the /attach action (api/panel/actions/attach or the bot /attach flow), not approve_student. Either move event (1) to the attach path or drop it; as specced it does nothing.

PANEL AUTH: TWO entry paths must BOTH be relaxed AND the spec misses that panel/auth/telegram is the Telegram Login Widget path. Verified gates: webhook /panel branch line ~1228 `!['admin','super_admin'].includes(prof.role)` and panel/auth/telegram line 39 `profile.role !== 'admin' && profile.role !== 'super_admin'`. Both reject curator today; both must add curator. Correct. But note layout.tsx currently allows ANY session (only `if(!session) redirect`) and calls countPendingRequests for everyone — the spec's plan to guard pendingCount for curators is right, but ALSO the (dash) layout itself does not gate to admin/super_admin (the prompt's claim 'layout.tsx gates to admin/super_admin' is inaccurate — it only gates on session existence). So a curator session already passes the layout; the real work is purely the per-page/per-route scoping. Good, but state it accurately.

stats-data.getPanelStats SCOPE PLUMBING — the byCity/byCountry/progress/streaks/stuck do derive from `students`, BUT note `students = visibleStudents(profiles, isOwner)` filters role==='student' only; you must add the curator_id filter INSIDE/around visibleStudents, not just on the final lists. Also totals.curators/totals.admins iterate ALL profiles (lines 151-154) independent of `students`, so for curator scope you must zero them explicitly (spec says this — good) but ALSO totals.cities is computed from activeCityIds over scoped students already, fine. Concretely add the scope as `const scoped = scopeCuratorId ? profiles.filter(p=>p.curator_id===scopeCuratorId) : profiles` BEFORE visibleStudents, and pass that. The spec's 'add a second arg scopeCuratorId' is correct but underspecifies that the curator/admin tallies are computed off the unfiltered profiles array and need separate handling.

curatorChatIdForStudent ROLE FILTER may silently drop legitimately-attached curators. Spec resolves telegram_chat_id only where role IN ('curator','admin','super_admin'). But profiles.curator_id can point at any profile and there is no DB guarantee the referenced profile currently has one of those roles (no FK role-check). If a student's curator_id points at someone whose role was changed, the push silently no-ops. That's acceptable-by-design but document it, and prefer selecting curator_id + telegram_chat_id in ONE query rather than two round-trips; ensure telegram_chat_id null/0 → no-op (the column is nullable).

FIRE-AND-FORGET (`void fireDayAndBlockEvents`) in serverless will be KILLED before completion. On Vercel, un-awaited async work after the response is returned is not guaranteed to run (the function may freeze/terminate). The spec's 'call fire-and-forget (not awaited blocking the user response)' will drop pushes intermittently. Use `await` (the Telegram call is ~100-300ms and dedupe makes it safe), or wrap with Next.js `after()` from next/server (App Router) which is the supported post-response hook. Do NOT rely on bare `void`/floating promise. Same applies to the onboarding curator push.

DUPLICATE-DETECTION RACE: spec correctly says use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and check rowCount, never SELECT-then-INSERT. But supabase-js does NOT return rowCount for .insert() by default — you must use .insert(row, {count:'exact'}) or chain .select() and check returned-rows length (a conflicted no-op returns zero rows). Ensure the helper checks `data?.length===1`, and that the table GRANT includes nothing beyond service_role (it does). Also the UNIQUE is (student_id, event_type, event_key) — for block_completed with event_key='block:'||N this dedupes per block number (good, survives accel virtual dates per RISK 2).

EFFECTIVE_DATE / ACCEL not in spec's data model. The recitation and location tables have an effective_date column (test_daily_accel mode stamps virtual dates), and ALL the RPCs key off COALESCE(effective_date, (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date). The spec's RISK 2 mentions accel/'virtual 2000-01-01 dates' but the day_closed event_key (the date string) must therefore be the COALESCE'd date, not real today — for accel testers, fireDayAndBlockEvents derived from real-today UTC would never match a virtual closed day. Since you should derive day_closed from the RPC delta (count increased) rather than computing a date yourself, this is another reason NOT to compute the closed-date in TS: just detect 'count went up' and use whatever closed-date the recompute exposes (or omit the exact date in copy).

specs.title: Inactivity (>3d) alert to curator + per-curator notification settings

spec.summary:
Feature = curator inactivity alerts + per-curator notification settings. Two parts: (1) a Bali-time Vercel cron that, for each curator, finds THEIR students (profiles.curator_id = curator.id) who have been inactive > threshold days and pushes a Telegram alert to the curator, honoring per-curator settings and deduping so it doesn't re-fire daily; (2) a new table `curator_notification_settings` + a settings UI.

Verified codebase facts that shape the design:
- Panel cookie-auth (lib/admin/session.ts) restricts to AdminRole='admin'|'super_admin'. Curators are blocked in THREE places: the bot `/panel` command (webhook/route.ts:1228), signLoginToken's role type, and panel/(dash)/layout.tsx. So "curator panel" does NOT exist yet.
- Curators DO already authenticate in MiniApp /m/* via requireCuratorViaInitData (lib/curator-auth.ts, X-Init-Data header). The lowest-risk place for the settings UI is therefore the MiniApp curator surface, NOT /panel. I designed the settings UI in /m/curator/settings (initData auth) as the PRIMARY path, and note the heavier "open panel to curators" alternative.
- notifications_log (migration 20260521200100) already has type 'silence_3days' and a UNIQUE(curator_id, student_id, block_id, notification_type, created_at::DATE). It is IN-APP ONLY (no Telegram). Its UNIQUE is unreliable for inactivity dedup because block_id is NULL for an inactivity alert and Postgres treats NULLs as distinct in unique indexes → no dedup. So dedup must be tracked separately (last_alerted_date on the settings/recipient row, NOT via notifications_log UNIQUE).
- Activity signal: student_daily_activity.opened (per Bali date), closed_dates_all() RPC (dates a student closed), profiles.last_active_date. "Active" = max(last opened date, last closed date). Inactive days = baliToday - lastActiveDate.
- Telegram send: lib/telegram/send.ts sendTelegramMessage(chatId, html, {withMiniAppButton|inlineKeyboard}). Curator chat id = profiles.telegram_chat_id. escapeHtml() for student names.
- Cron pattern: Bearer CRON_SECRET, baliToday(), vercel.json crons (currently only daily-reminder 18/21).
spec.dataModel:
NEW migration: supabase/migrations/20260621100000_v3_curator_notification_settings.sql

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS curator_notification_settings (
  curator_id              UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  -- event toggles (default ON to preserve current behavior)
  notify_new_submission   BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  notify_block_exam_ready BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  notify_exam_passed      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  notify_inactivity       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  -- inactivity tuning
  inactivity_threshold_days INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 3
        CHECK (inactivity_threshold_days BETWEEN 1 AND 30),
  -- delivery cadence for inactivity alerts
  digest_frequency        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'daily'
        CHECK (digest_frequency IN ('immediate','daily','off')),
  -- mute Telegram entirely (in-app notifications_log still written)
  telegram_enabled        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Per (curator, student) dedup ledger for inactivity alerts. Separate table so
-- dedup works even though notifications_log.block_id is NULL (NULLs are distinct
-- in Postgres unique indexes → notifications_log UNIQUE can't dedup inactivity).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS curator_inactivity_alerts (
  curator_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  student_id          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  last_alerted_date   DATE NOT NULL,        -- Bali date the alert last fired
  last_inactive_days  INTEGER NOT NULL,     -- streak length when last alerted
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  PRIMARY KEY (curator_id, student_id)
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_cia_curator ON curator_inactivity_alerts(curator_id);

Notes:
- No new columns on profiles. Reuse profiles.curator_id, profiles.telegram_chat_id, profiles.last_active_date, profiles.hidden_from_tracking (skip hidden students).
- digest_frequency='immediate' vs 'daily': both are evaluated by ONE daily cron; 'immediate' has no extra meaning beyond 'daily' unless a second high-frequency cron is added — modeled now, wired as alias of 'daily' in v1 (documented in risks). 'off' = never send inactivity Telegram.
- Backfill: cron treats a MISSING settings row as all-defaults (no need to pre-insert rows for every curator). A trigger to auto-seed is optional; the cron's COALESCE-on-missing approach avoids it.
spec.apiRoutes:
1) CRON — apps/web/src/app/api/cron/curator-inactivity/route.ts  (GET)
   Auth: Authorization: Bearer ${CRON_SECRET} (same guard as daily-reminder/route.ts).
   Schedule: vercel.json add { "path": "/api/cron/curator-inactivity", "schedule": "0 14 * * *" } = 22:00 Bali (after the 21:00 student reminder, so today's activity is final).
   Logic:
   - supabase = createServiceSupabase(); today = baliToday().
   - Load all students with a curator: profiles where role='student', curator_id NOT NULL, hidden_from_tracking != true, selecting id, full_name, curator_id, last_active_date.
   - Compute lastActive per student = max( last_active_date, last student_daily_activity.opened date, last closed_dates_all() date ). Build maps once: query student_daily_activity (max activity_date where opened) grouped, and SELECT * FROM closed_dates_all() into Map<user, maxDate>. inactiveDays = diff(today, lastActive). Students with no activity ever and course_started_at older than threshold also count.
   - Load curators referenced: profiles in (curatorIds) selecting id, telegram_chat_id; left-join curator_notification_settings (one query, settingsByCurator map; missing → defaults).
   - For each student where settings.notify_inactivity AND settings.digest_frequency != 'off' AND inactiveDays >= settings.inactivity_threshold_days:
       * DEDUP: read curator_inactivity_alerts row. Skip if last_alerted_date == today (already alerted today) OR (last_alerted_date present AND inactiveDays == last_inactive_days, i.e. no change since last alert — avoids daily spam while streak is unchanged). Re-alert only when the streak GREW or it's been >=7 days since last alert (re-nag cadence). When student becomes active again, lastActive moves → inactiveDays resets → no alert; optionally delete the ledger row.
       * Group alerts per curator (digest): collect [{name, inactiveDays}].
   - Send: per curator, if telegram_enabled and telegram_chat_id present, sendTelegramMessage(chatId, digest html with escapeHtml(name), {withMiniAppButton:false / inlineKeyboard to /panel или /m}). One message per curator listing all inactive students (digest semantics), e.g. "⚠️ 2 ученика молчат 3+ дн.: Имя (4 дн.), Имя (3 дн.)".
   - Upsert curator_inactivity_alerts {curator_id, student_id, last_alerted_date: today, last_inactive_days: inactiveDays} for each alerted student.
   - Also INSERT notifications_log {curator_id, student_id, block_id: null, notification_type:'silence_3days'} for the in-app feed (best-effort; do NOT rely on its UNIQUE for dedup).
   - Return {ok, date, curatorsAlerted, studentsFlagged}.

2) SETTINGS API (PRIMARY, MiniApp auth) — apps/web/src/app/api/curator/settings/route.ts
   GET: requireCuratorViaInitData(req.headers 'x-init-data') → return current settings row or defaults.
   PUT/PATCH: validate body (booleans; inactivity_threshold_days 1..30; digest_frequency in set) → upsert curator_notification_settings with curator_id = authed userId. Never trust curator_id from body.
   dynamic = 'force-dynamic'. Errors in {error:{code,message}} shape per existing convention.

ALTERNATIVE (if settings must live in cookie /panel): apps/web/src/app/api/panel/settings/notifications/route.ts using getPanelSessionFromReq(req); requires first extending panel auth to curators (see UI section, larger effort).
spec.ui: PRIMARY (MiniApp curator surface, initData auth — matches existing /m/* + requireCuratorViaInitData): - ADD apps/web/src/app/m/curator/settings/page.tsx ('use client'): fetch GET /api/curator/settings with X-Init-Data, render toggles (Switch) for notify_new_submission / notify_block_exam_ready / notify_exam_passed / notify_inactivity, a number/slider for inactivity_threshold_days (1..30, default 3), a select for digest_frequency (Мгновенно/Раз в день/Выключено), and telegram_enabled toggle. Save → PUT, toast() success. <200 lines; split form into apps/web/src/app/m/curator/settings/SettingsForm.tsx if needed. - ADD a link to settings in the existing curator MiniApp entry (wherever curator notifications screen lives). All strings Russian. ALTERNATIVE (true /panel, larger): to put settings under /panel/(dash)/settings you must FIRST open the panel to curators — - session.ts: widen AdminRole to include 'curator' (ripples to layout.tsx gate, signLoginToken, webhook /panel command at route.ts:1228 which currently rejects non-admins). - ADD panel/(dash)/settings/page.tsx + SettingsForm client. - ADD nav link in PanelNav.tsx LINKS. This touches auth-critical code (3 gates) and is explicitly more invasive — recommend deferring; do MiniApp version for v1. Inactivity Telegram message copy (curator-facing, Russian, escapeHtml on names): "⚠️ Молчание учеников\n\n• Имя — 4 дн. без активности\n• Имя — 3 дн.\n\nНапомни им заглянуть в КРЕСТ 🙏"
implementationOrder: 1. Migration 20260621100000_v3_curator_notification_settings.sql: create curator_notification_settings + curator_inactivity_alerts, RLS policies, indexes (IF NOT EXISTS everywhere). Apply via /run-migration skill.
2. Regenerate types.ts and run tsc --noEmit (MEMORY rule: regen types after ADD COLUMN/TABLE or Vercel build silently breaks).

3. lib/curator/inactivity.ts helper: computeLastActive(student) over last_active_date + student_daily_activity max opened date + closed_dates_all() map; pure functions, easy to unit-reason. Keep <200 lines.
4. lib/curator/settings.ts: loadSettings(supabase, curatorIds) → Map with defaults for missing rows; getSettingsForCurator helper used by both cron and API.
5. API: app/api/curator/settings/route.ts (GET/PUT via requireCuratorViaInitData).
6. Cron: app/api/cron/curator-inactivity/route.ts (Bearer CRON_SECRET, baliToday, dedup via curator_inactivity_alerts, digest send, notifications_log insert).
7. vercel.json: add cron entry { path:/api/cron/curator-inactivity, schedule:'0 14 * * *' } (22:00 Bali).
8. UI: app/m/curator/settings/page.tsx + SettingsForm.tsx; add entry link from curator notifications screen.
9. Manual test: set a test student's last_active_date back >3d, hit the cron URL locally with the Bearer secret, confirm one Telegram digest + ledger row + no second send on a repeat run same day.
10. tsc --noEmit + npm run lint + build before deploy.
spec.risks:
- DEDUP CORRECTNESS (highest): notifications_log UNIQUE includes block_id which is NULL for inactivity → Postgres treats NULLs as distinct → that constraint does NOT dedup. Must dedup via curator_inactivity_alerts.last_alerted_date, not via notifications_log. Re-alert policy needs care: alert once when threshold first crossed, re-nag only when streak grows or after a cooldown (e.g. >=7d), reset/delete ledger row when student becomes active — else either daily spam or alert-never-repeats.
- TIMEZONE: whole platform is Bali-fixed (baliToday); cities.timezone exists but is unused (confirmed in block-status route). 'Active today' uses Bali date boundaries consistently, so no per-student tz drift for v1 — but document that inactiveDays is Bali-day granularity.
- ACTIVITY DEFINITION ambiguity: 'opened' (just launched app) vs 'closed a day' (did the work) are different. Using max(opened, closed, last_active_date) means a student who only opens the app but does nothing is counted active → may hide real inactivity. Decide with Михаил: recommend 'opened' as the activity signal for v1 (matches the student daily-reminder semantics) and note 'closed' as a stricter future option.
- digest_frequency 'immediate' has no distinct behavior under a single daily cron; in v1 it aliases 'daily'. True immediate alerts would need event-time hooks (on submission-miss) — out of scope; documented.
- PANEL vs MINIAPP: prompt says 'curator panel' but the cookie /panel is admin-only in 3 auth-critical spots. Building settings in /m/* (initData) avoids touching auth gates. Flagged as a spec/code conflict to confirm before widening AdminRole.
- SCALE/perf: cron loads all curated students + closed_dates_all() (full-table RPC). Fine at launch scale (Bali only); add per-curator batching if student count grows.
- NULL telegram_chat_id curators (whitelisted but never opened bot) silently get no Telegram — correct, but ensure in-app notifications_log still written so they see it on next login.
- escapeHtml MUST wrap student names in the Telegram digest (names are user-controlled) or a name with < > breaks parse_mode=HTML and the message is dropped.

critique.verdict: SOUND CORE DESIGN, BUT SHIP-BLOCKING CORRECTNESS GAPS. The architecture is well-reasoned and most codebase claims check out (requireCuratorViaInitData exists, is_admin() exists, notifications_log/silence_3days exists, Bali=UTC+8 so the 0 14 * * * cron = 22:00 Bali is arithmetically correct, closed_dates_all() returns (user_id, d)). But four categories of error must be fixed before implementation: (1) a FALSE premise that the settings table "preserves current behavior" for submission/exam toggles — those curator Telegram pushes DO NOT EXIST today; (2) a genuine timezone bug from mixing UTC-stamped last_active_date with baliToday(); (3) a broken/misleading RLS design for the MiniApp (initData) auth model where auth.uid() does not resolve the curator; (4) wrong claim about the notifications_log UNIQUE constraint. Corrections below, ordered by severity.
corrections: FALSE PREMISE — notify_new_submission / notify_block_exam_ready / notify_exam_passed do NOT correspond to any existing Telegram-to-curator code. Verified: the ONLY curator-targeted Telegram sends are notifyAdmins/getAdminChatIds in /api/m/location-request and /api/m/curator-request (those go to ADMINS, not the student's curator). approve/route.ts only marks notifications_log read; exam/submit just sets final_exam_passed_at. So modeling three toggles whose DEFAULT TRUE 'preserves current behavior' is wrong — there is no current behavior to preserve; these toggles would silently do nothing until separate features are built. Fix: scope v1 strictly to notify_inactivity + telegram_enabled (+ threshold/digest), drop or clearly mark the other three as 'reserved, not wired' so a future dev doesn't assume they gate live pushes.

TIMEZONE BUG (real, not cosmetic) — spec computes inactiveDays = baliToday() - lastActive where lastActive includes profiles.last_active_date. But last_active_date is written in /api/miniapp/streak/route.ts as `new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10)` = UTC date (CURRENT_DATE semantics), NOT baliToday(). closed_dates_all() recitation rows likewise use `(created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date`. Mixing a UTC-derived 'last active' with a Bali 'today' produces a guaranteed off-by-one for the 8h window 16:00–24:00 UTC (00:00–08:00 Bali): a student active 'today Bali' can read as +1 inactive day. With threshold=3 and the cron at 22:00 Bali, this risks false 3-day alerts. Fix: normalize all activity dates to ONE clock. Either convert last_active_date to Bali at read-time, or (cleaner) derive lastActive purely from student_daily_activity.opened (which IS keyed on Bali activity_date per daily-reminder route) + closed_dates_all, and treat last_active_date as advisory only. Document the chosen clock explicitly.

RLS / AUTH-MODEL MISMATCH on curator_notification_settings — the policy `curator_id = auth.uid()` is effectively DEAD for the primary (MiniApp) path. requireCuratorViaInitData returns a SERVICE-role client (createServiceSupabase, RLS bypassed) and enforces ownership in CODE; MiniApp users authenticate via X-Init-Data, and the supabase auth session (telegram-auth route) is built on the ANON key with a noted 'service_role в Vercel некорректный' caveat. So in practice auth.uid() will not be the curator in these requests. The RLS policy isn't harmful (it satisfies the 'RLS on every table' rule and guards any future authed path) but the spec's claim that 'RLS is the second line of defense' overstates it for this surface. Fix: keep the policies, but the REAL guarantee 'a curator never reads/writes another curator's settings' MUST come from code: in PUT/PATCH set curator_id := auth.curator.userId and NEVER from body (spec says this — good), and in GET filter .eq('curator_id', authedUserId). Make that non-negotiable in the implementation order, and add a note that the auth.uid() policies are latent until cookie-auth covers curators.

DEDUP / notifications_log UNIQUE claim is INACCURATE — spec says the UNIQUE 'includes block_id which is NULL → NULLs distinct → does NOT dedup'. The actual constraint is UNIQUE(curator_id, student_id, block_id, notification_type, created_at::DATE). The decision to use a SEPARATE curator_inactivity_alerts ledger is CORRECT and good, but the stated reason is half-wrong: with block_id NULL the row is NOT 'never deduped' — created_at::DATE already gives one-insert-per-day-per-(curator,student,type) for non-null columns, and a NULL block_id is consistently NULL so two inserts the same day DO collide on the rest... except Postgres treats NULL as distinct in UNIQUE, so the collision is NOT enforced. Net effect matches spec's conclusion (don't rely on it) but for the precise reason 'NULL block_id makes the unique index non-enforcing', and also note the INSERT into notifications_log can RAISE a duplicate-key error on a same-day re-run for any non-null-block path — wrap it best-effort (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or ignore error) so a retry of the cron doesn't 500.

ACTIVITY DEFINITION must be locked before coding, not deferred — spec's lastActive = max(opened, closed, last_active_date) means a student who merely opens the MiniApp (opened=true) but does ZERO coursework resets the inactivity clock, hiding real disengagement. This directly conflicts with the feature's intent (curator wants to know who STOPPED doing the work). The daily-reminder cron uses 'opened' semantics, but for an INACTIVITY-to-curator alert 'closed a day' (real work) is the more honest signal. This is a product decision (flagged for Михаил in the spec, good) but it changes the data sources and the query, so it cannot be 'decided later' — pick one for v1 and wire only that source.

MIGRATION SAFETY — mostly fine (IF NOT EXISTS everywhere, no destructive DDL, CASCADE FKs to profiles are appropriate, CHECK constraints sound). Two nits: (a) curator_notification_settings has updated_at but no trigger to maintain it — either add a BEFORE UPDATE trigger (project has none generically; set it in the upsert code instead and say so) or drop the column to avoid a stale field. (b) The spec lists `cns_upsert_own` in the DROP line but then creates `cns_insert_own` — the DROP POLICY IF EXISTS name must match the CREATE name, otherwise the old policy (if any) isn't dropped on re-run; align the names. Also explicitly GRANT/REVOKE is unnecessary since writes go through service_role, but confirm no anon/authenticated INSERT policy is added to curator_inactivity_alerts (spec correctly omits it — good, keep it service-role-only).

CRON SCHEDULE NOTE — the 0 14 * * * = 22:00 Bali claim is CORRECT (verified: existing 18:00 Bali reminder = 0 10 * * * UTC, Bali=UTC+8, no DST per lib/time/bali.ts). No change needed, but vercel.json lives at apps/web/vercel.json (confirmed) and currently encodes stage via querystring (?stage=18). Keep the new entry's path query-less and ensure the route reads no required query param. Also the existing crons use stage=18/20/21 at 10/12/13 UTC — adding 14 UTC is non-conflicting.

MINOR — escapeHtml on student names in the digest is correctly flagged (names are user-controlled, parse_mode=HTML). Good. One addition: also guard against a curator with NULL telegram_chat_id (whitelisted but never opened the bot) — spec handles this (still writes notifications_log) but make sure the cron does not attempt sendTelegramMessage(null, ...) and that 'studentsFlagged' in the return still counts them so the run is observable.

specs.title: Curator focus on students + report generation by selected students

spec.summary:
Two related features for КРЕСТ панель (/panel) and curators.

(1) Curator "focus" — a curator (or admin) can pin specific of their students as "в фокусе". Stored in a new table curator_student_focus (curator_id, student_id, note). Surfaced as a star toggle on the panel students list and student detail, and consumed in the existing curator notification feed (notifications_log) so focused students float to the top + get Telegram push to the curator. Requires letting curators (read-only, scoped) into /panel for the first time.

(2) Report by SELECTED students — extend the admin-only GenerateReport into a real per-student report. Admins pick from ALL visible students; curators pick from THEIR students only (profiles.curator_id = self). A new server route POST /api/panel/report assembles per-student progress (block-by-block closed days, quiz, passed count, last activity, focus flag, stuck status) and returns text; client offers copy / download .txt / print-to-PDF.

CRITICAL existing-codebase facts that constrain the design:
- /panel runs ENTIRELY on service-role (RLS bypassed). Authorization is the krest_admin HMAC cookie (AdminSession{uid,role}) gated to admin|super_admin in 4 places. So "curator limited to own students" must be enforced IN CODE (filter by curator_id===session.uid), NOT by RLS — RLS on the new table is a defense-in-depth backstop only.
- AdminRole type is 'admin'|'super_admin' ONLY. Adding curator to the panel means widening AdminRole to include 'curator' (session.ts), and widening the 4 gates. This is the biggest blast-radius change and must be done carefully so existing admin-only pages stay admin-only.
- Per-student data is already computable from passed_blocks_all(), closed_dates_all(), user_closed_days(p_user_id), student_block_progress — reuse, do not reinvent.
spec.dataModel:
NEW MIGRATION: supabase/migrations/20260621100000_v3_curator_student_focus.sql

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.curator_student_focus (
  id          uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  curator_id  uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  student_id  uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  note        text,                       -- короткая заметка куратора (опц., <=500 симв., обрезать в API)
  created_at  timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  created_by  uuid REFERENCES public.profiles(id), -- кто поставил фокус (куратор или админ)
  CONSTRAINT curator_student_focus_uniq UNIQUE (curator_id, student_id)
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_csf_curator ON public.curator_student_focus(curator_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_csf_student ON public.curator_student_focus(student_id);

Design notes:
- Keyed on (curator_id, student_id), NOT a boolean on profiles, because focus is per-curator-relationship and admins may pin a student on behalf of that student's curator. The natural curator is profiles.curator_id of the student; admins setting focus pass curator_id = the student's current curator_id (reject if student has no curator -> 422 "у ученика нет куратора").
- "note" is curator-authored content; in report/UI render via React auto-escape (textContent), never innerHTML.
- ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY (table is normally accessed via service-role which bypasses RLS, but RLS is mandatory per project rules — policies below are a backstop for any future anon/auth-client access).

NO column added to profiles. NO schema change needed for the report feature — it reads existing tables/RPCs.

After migration: regenerate apps/web/src/lib/types.ts (mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types) + run tsc --noEmit (per memory feedback_regen_types_after_migration).
spec.apiRoutes: All panel routes auth via getPanelSessionFromReq(req) + service-role client. "scope to own" enforced in code. 1) POST /api/panel/focus (toggle/set focus) Auth: curator | admin | super_admin (cookie session). Body: { studentId: string, on: boolean, note?: string } Logic: - Load student profile (id, curator_id, role='student', hidden_from_tracking). - If session.role === 'curator': require student.curator_id === session.uid else 403. curatorId = session.uid. - If admin/super_admin: curatorId = student.curator_id; if null -> 422 {error:'У ученика нет куратора'}. - on=true -> upsert into curator_student_focus (curator_id, student_id, note=trim(note).slice(0,500), created_by=session.uid) ON CONFLICT (curator_id,student_id) DO UPDATE note. - on=false -> delete where curator_id+student_id. Returns { ok:true, focused:boolean }. 2) GET /api/panel/focus (list focus ids for current viewer's scope) Auth: curator|admin|super_admin. Returns { ok:true, ids: string[] } where ids = student_ids in focus for: curator -> their own rows; admin/super_admin -> ALL rows (so star shows globally). Used to hydrate the students list + detail star state. 3) POST /api/panel/report (NEW — the selected-students report) Auth: curator|admin|super_admin. Body: { studentIds: string[] (1..200), includeFocusNotes?: boolean } Logic: - Determine allowed set: admin/super_admin -> all visible students (reuse visibility rule: isOwner via resolveIsOwner else exclude hidden_from_tracking); curator -> only profiles where curator_id===session.uid. - Intersect requested studentIds with allowed set; silently drop disallowed ids (do NOT 403 the whole request, but include count of dropped in response meta). - For the resulting ids assemble per-student data (see dataModel of report below) in BULK: * profiles (name, contact_info, city via cities(name_ru), curator_id, course_started_at) * passed_blocks_all() -> passedBlocks map * closed_dates_all() -> total closed days + last closed date (for stuck calc, Bali-day index) * per requested user: user_closed_days(p_user_id) for block breakdown; student_block_progress for quiz_passed_at/updated_at * curator_student_focus rows in scope -> focus flag + note - Returns { ok:true, generatedAt, scope:'admin'|'curator', students: ReportStudent[], droppedCount }. Note: keep route <200 lines by extracting assembly into apps/web/src/lib/admin/report-data.ts (mirrors stats-data.ts pattern). 4) STUDENT LIST PROVIDER for curators: Reuse existing POST /api/panel/students but it currently returns ALL students. Add scoping: if session.role==='curator', filter rows to curator_id===session.uid AND restrict the returned `curators` dropdown to [] (curators cannot reassign). Add `focused:boolean` to each PanelStudentRow (join GET focus ids server-side or in the same handler). 5) Telegram push for focused students (extend, not new route): In apps/web/src/app/api/curator/submissions/[id]/approve/route.ts and the submission-created notification path (wherever notifications_log rows are inserted for a curator), after insert: if a curator_student_focus row exists for (curator_id, student_id) AND curator has telegram_chat_id, send sendTelegramMessage(curatorChatId, '⭐ {name} (в фокусе): {event}'). Best-effort, catch-and-ignore. Add helper apps/web/src/lib/curator/focus-notify.ts.
