spec.risks:
EDGE CASES & RISKS:
1. AUTHORIZATION blast radius (highest): /panel is service-role, so widening AdminRole to 'curator' instantly grants curators access to EVERY existing panel route unless each is re-gated. The pre-existing POST /api/panel/students and GET /api/panel/student/[id] currently return ALL students with only a `if(!session)` check — a curator session would see everyone. MUST add curator-scoping to BOTH before shipping, or this leaks all students to any curator. This is the #1 review item.
2. Student detail [id] for curators: must verify ownership (student.curator_id===session.uid) or 403, else a curator could read any student by guessing UUID.
3. hidden_from_tracking: report + focus must keep owner-only visibility (resolveIsOwner) for admins; curators only see own students anyway.
4. Admin sets focus on a student with no curator -> 422 (no curator_id to attach to). Decide UX: maybe allow admin focus keyed to admin themselves? Recommend reject + toast "сначала назначьте куратора".
5. note is curator free-text -> XSS: render via React text only, trim/cap 500 chars server-side.
6. Report size: 200 students x per-user user_closed_days() RPC = 200 round-trips. Mitigate: cap selection at 200, and prefer ONE closed_dates_all() pass for totals + only call user_closed_days per-student if block breakdown requested; or add a future bulk RPC. For first cut, sequential with a hard cap is acceptable (admin-triggered, infrequent).
7. Curator reaching /panel for the first time: BotFather /setdomain + login token role widening; an existing curator who taps /panel before deploy gets "только администраторам" — fine.
8. PDF: true PDF lib (jsPDF) is heavier; window.print to "Save as PDF" is the cheap, dependency-free path and matches existing .txt/copy approach. Cyrillic in jsPDF needs font embedding — another reason to prefer print.
9. ON CONFLICT upsert needs the UNIQUE(curator_id,student_id) constraint — ensure it lands before any insert.
10. RLS policies use auth.uid()/profiles subquery but panel never hits them (service-role); don't let a reviewer "fix" curator-scoping by relying on these — scoping lives in API code.

critique.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES — the feature is sound and the spec correctly identifies the #1 risk (service-role panel = code-level scoping, not RLS), but it ships several factual mismatches with the actual code and a load-bearing timezone/day-model error that would make the report numbers wrong and inconsistent with the rest of the platform. Fix the corrections below before implementation.
corrections: TIMEZONE (load-bearing, report-correctness): The spec says the day-close model 'CURRENTLY uses UTC date boundaries, NOT student timezone' and frames it as background context — but it does NOT instruct the report to account for this, and it must. The actual RPCs the report reuses (user_closed_days, closed_dates_all, passed_blocks_all in supabase/migrations/20260620160000_v3_mestopisaniya_daily.sql) compute recitation/location dates as (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date — UTC, while cross/prayer use stored DATE columns (submitted_date/prayed_date) that are written client-side as Bali dates. So 'closed days' already MIXES UTC-derived and Bali-derived dates; a recitation done at 02:00 Bali (=18:00 prev-day UTC) lands on the wrong calendar day and may fail to INTERSECT the same-day cross/prayer. The report will faithfully reproduce this bug and surface wrong X/7 counts. The spec must either (a) explicitly state the report inherits the known UTC skew and is 'eventually consistent', or (b) recommend fixing the RPCs to use cities.timezone (the column exists, is unused) — do NOT silently present these counts as authoritative in an admin/curator report.

DAY-MODEL FACTUAL ERROR: The spec's 'Key existing pieces' says a day is closed when 'cross+prayer+recitation(audio)+mestopisaniya(video_note)+quiz done same date' (5 sources incl. quiz). This is WRONG on two counts vs the live RPCs: (1) the model uses 4 sources, HAVING count(DISTINCT src)=4 — cross, prayer, recitation-audio, locations(video_note) — quiz/trainer/friday were DROPPED from the daily gate (migrations 20260620130000_v3_drop_quiz_from_gate, 20260620140000_v3_drop_trainer_from_gate). (2) Quiz is a one-time per-block flag (quiz_passed_at), NOT part of the daily close. The report's per-block 'X/7 дней + квиз ✓/—' formatting is fine, but any assembly code or reviewer reasoning built on the spec's '5 sources incl. quiz' premise will be wrong. Correct the premise.

WRONG FILE PATH for student detail edits: The spec's Focus UI section says to add the focus star + note textarea to 'students/[id]/StudentDetailClient.tsx', and risk #2 references a curator reading 'any student by guessing UUID'. Verified: the data route is GET /api/panel/student/[id]/route.ts and it currently has ONLY `if (!session)` — it returns ANY student profile + full block breakdown by UUID with NO curator-ownership check. This is a real IDOR once curators enter the panel. The spec lists scoping students/[id] but buries it; it MUST be a hard blocker line: add assertOwnsStudent (student.curator_id===session.uid) at the TOP of GET /api/panel/student/[id]/route.ts before any data load, returning 403/404, AND in the students/[id]/page.tsx Server Component. (The approve route path the spec gives — api/curator/submissions/[id]/approve/route.ts — IS correct; that one verified fine.)

IN-APP FEED PRIORITIZATION conflicts with actual auth architecture: The spec says focused students 'consumed in the existing curator notification feed (notifications_log) so they float to the top'. But GET /api/curator/notifications uses requireCuratorAuth (Telegram initData via X-Init-Data header), NOT the krest_admin panel cookie session this whole feature is built on. These are two SEPARATE auth systems and two separate clients. A focus row written from /panel (cookie session) cannot be trivially 'floated to top' in the initData-authed miniapp feed without joining curator_student_focus there too. Either drop the in-app-feed prioritization (spec already marks it 'optional, low priority' — make it explicitly out-of-scope) or specify the join in the curator/notifications route. As written it implies a coupling that doesn't exist.

notifications_log CANNOT carry a 'focus' event as specified: notification_type is a CHECK-constrained enum (new_submission, silence_1day, silence_3days, block_exam_ready, exam_passed) with UNIQUE(curator_id,student_id,block_id,notification_type,created_at::DATE) — see migration 20260521200100_v3_curator_notifications_log.sql. The spec's Telegram-push-for-focused-students hooks into EXISTING insert points (approve/create) and only sends an extra sendTelegramMessage — that part is fine and needs no enum change. But any reviewer/implementer who reads 'consumed in notifications_log so focused float to top' may try to INSERT a focus-type row and hit the CHECK violation. State explicitly: NO new notification_type, NO new notifications_log rows for focus; focus only (a) reorders existing rows and (b) adds a best-effort Telegram push.

POST /api/panel/students 'add focused flag + scope curator' is bigger than spec admits: the route returns ALL profiles (needed to build the curator name-map at line 82-83) and ALL students with only `if(!session)`. To scope curators you must keep loading all profiles for the name map but FILTER the returned `students` rows to curator_id===session.uid AND return `curators:[]` for curator role. Also PanelStudentRow is imported by studentBits.tsx (line 5) and StudentRowActions — adding `focused:boolean` ripples to those consumers and to studentsSort.ts. Spec should note the type-change blast radius (3+ client files), not just 'add focused:boolean'.

RLS policy should reuse is_admin(), not an inline profiles subquery: an is_admin() SECURITY DEFINER helper already exists (migration 20260501130000_v3_roles_hierarchy.sql) and is the project's standard (used by notifications_log RLS). The spec's csf_admin_all policy uses `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles p WHERE p.id = auth.uid() AND p.role IN (...))` inline. Since profiles itself has RLS, an inline subquery from a policy can recurse / be blocked; is_admin() is SECURITY DEFINER and avoids that. Replace the inline EXISTS with is_admin(). (These policies are backstop-only per the spec since /panel uses service_role, but if they're written they must be written correctly.)

Migration safety is mostly OK but note pgcrypto/gen_random_uuid: gen_random_uuid() is used across existing migrations so the extension is present — fine. Table/index use IF NOT EXISTS — fine, no data loss. BUT the spec's ON CONFLICT (curator_id,student_id) upsert depends on the UNIQUE constraint named curator_student_focus_uniq; the migration defines it inline as a table CONSTRAINT (good), and risk #9 flags ordering — confirm the CREATE POLICY / GRANT statements are NOT re-run-fragile: wrap policies in DROP POLICY IF EXISTS ... before CREATE POLICY (the codebase convention — see notifications_log migration), otherwise a re-applied migration throws 'policy already exists'. The spec's RLS block omits the DROP POLICY IF EXISTS guards that every other migration uses.

Per memory feedback_regen_types_after_migration: spec correctly calls for regenerating types.ts + tsc --noEmit. Reinforce as a HARD gate before step 2 (widening AdminRole) — because widening AdminRole='admin'|'super_admin'|'curator' in session.ts and removing the `as 'admin'|'super_admin'` cast at webhook line ~1235 and auth/telegram line 39 will surface tsc errors across every consumer of AdminSession.role; build will pass locally only after both the type regen AND the cast removals land together, else Vercel build breaks silently (the exact failure mode that memory note warns about).

GenerateReport rewrite is a prop-signature change, not just a UI swap: current GenerateReport({stats: PanelStats}) is SERVER-FED (page.tsx passes pre-rendered stats, line ~63). The spec's new fetch-based picker (POST /api/panel/students → POST /api/panel/report) makes it self-fetching, so the `stats` prop and its import from stats-data must be removed and page.tsx updated. Also the spec wants this button on the students page header 'for all roles' AND kept on Обзор for admins — but curators are redirected away from /panel (Обзор) per the nav plan, so ensure the students-header report button does NOT leak the admin Обзор GenerateReport (which still receives full-platform PanelStats and would expose all-students stats to a curator). Keep the Обзор GenerateReport admin-only and give curators ONLY the per-student picker variant.

Admin-sets-focus-with-no-curator (422) UX: spec risk #4 is correct to reject. Recommend the chosen path: reject with 422 'У ученика нет куратора — сначала назначьте куратора' (do not key focus to the admin themselves — that pollutes the per-curator-relationship model and would show the star to no curator). Confirm this is the decided behavior, not left open.

PanelNav is a static `const LINKS` array with a hardcoded role label ('super_admin'?'Супер-админ':'Админ', line 78) — there is NO roleLinks(role) function yet. The spec says 'drive via a roleLinks(role) filter' as if it exists; it must be CREATED. For curator: filter LINKS to [Ученики (+report)], hide Обзор/Заявки/Кураторы/Города, and add a third role label branch ('curator'→'Куратор'). Minor, but the spec phrases it as a tweak rather than new code.

specs.title: Per-student timezone day-boundaries + day-completion next-day unlock + notification

spec.dataModel: New migration: supabase/migrations/20260621120000_v3_student_timezone_daygate.sql. 1. profiles.timezone (single source of truth, immune to later city edits). ALTER TABLE public.profiles ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS timezone TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT Asia/Makassar. Backfill keeping Bali default where no city or tz: UPDATE profiles p SET timezone=c.timezone FROM cities c WHERE p.city_id=c.id AND c.timezone NOT IN (empty, UTC) AND p.timezone=Asia/Makassar. Rationale: cities.timezone is NOT NULL DEFAULT UTC with valid IANA values (Asia/Makassar, Europe/Moscow, America/Los_Angeles). Copying onto the profile means later city CRUD or a student changing city does not silently re-bucket already-stamped days. NULL city keeps Asia/Makassar. 2. user_tz(p_user_id) returns TEXT. SECURITY DEFINER STABLE; returns COALESCE(NULLIF(timezone,empty),Asia/Makassar) from profiles. One shared tz definition for all RPCs. 3. is_day_closed(p_user_id,p_block_id,p_local_date) returns BOOLEAN (NEW canonical predicate). Centralizes the 4-src rule duplicated across 4 functions. True when for that local date the user has: cross (submitted_date=local_date), prayer (prayed_date=local_date), recitation audio passed where COALESCE(effective_date,(created_at AT TIME ZONE user_tz)::date)=local_date, and DISTINCT passed video_note locations at least the required count (same tz conversion). cross/prayer DATE columns are local-stamped after the insert-route fix so need no conversion. 4. Rewrite the 3 aggregate RPCs plus closed-day is_block_unlocked. user_closed_days(p_user_id) and is_block_unlocked know the user, so substitute (created_at AT TIME ZONE public.user_tz(p_user_id))::date for both UTC literals. closed_dates_all() and passed_blocks_all() scan all users, so JOIN profiles and use (... AT TIME ZONE COALESCE(NULLIF(p.timezone,empty),Asia/Makassar))::date. Keep count(DISTINCT src)=4. Re-create ALL with the 4-src rule, NOT the older 5-src trainer version in the _helper migrations, so an older definition cannot win on re-apply. 5. day_progress(p_user_id,p_block_id) returns (cross,prayer,pereskaz,mestopisaniya booleans) so block-status avoids TZ math in TS. 6. notifications_log dedup (NO new table). Student-log shape has user_id, channel, type, payload jsonb, status. Add partial unique index: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_notif_day_counted ON notifications_log (user_id,(payload to block_id),(payload to local_date)) WHERE type=day_counted. COLLISION WARNING: two migrations define notifications_log (20260426120002 student log with payload; 20260521200100 curator log with curator_id). Verify live columns before writing payload or dedup. All functions SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path=empty with REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC and GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role. No money fields. No new tables.
spec.apiRoutes: All MiniApp routes already auth via resolveUserId(initData) (Telegram HMAC plus whitelist); no auth change. RPC EXECUTE stays service_role-only. CHANGED write routes (stamp local date plus event-notify): 1. apps/web/src/app/api/m/cross-photo/upload/route.ts (POST multipart) - replace todayDateStr() UTC with localToday(tz); add timezone and telegram_chat_id to the existing profile select; keep accel (virtual 2000-01-01) branch untouched; after upsert and count call maybeNotifyDayClosed. 2. apps/web/src/app/api/m/prayer/[blockId]/route.ts (POST) - todayStr and markDate via localToday(tz); add timezone and telegram_chat_id to existing profile select; event-notify after mark upsert. 3. apps/web/src/app/api/m/recitation/upload/route.ts (POST multipart) - audio path: no DATE column (gate converts created_at via tz on RPC side); add event-notify after a successful audio pass. 4. apps/web/src/app/api/m/locations/upload/route.ts (POST multipart) - video_note path: gate converts created_at via tz on RPC side; add event-notify after a passing video_note. CHANGED read route: 5. apps/web/src/app/api/m/block-status/[blockId]/route.ts (POST) - remove module todayUTC(); fetch tz; either compute today plus the recitation/location created_at gte/lt UTC bounds via localDateBoundsUtc, OR replace the 4 today-checks with the day_progress RPC (simpler). closedDays already from user_closed_days (now tz-correct). NEW libs (no new HTTP route): 6. apps/web/src/lib/time/student-tz.ts (under 60 lines): localToday(tz) via Intl.DateTimeFormat(en-CA,timeZone tz) giving YYYY-MM-DD; localDateBoundsUtc(tz,dateStr) giving gteUtc and ltUtc. Mirrors lib/time/bali.ts style. 7. apps/web/src/lib/daygate/notify-day-closed.ts (under 70 lines): maybeNotifyDayClosed(supabase,userId,blockId,localDate,chatId): rpc is_day_closed; if false return; INSERT notifications_log type day_counted payload block_id and local_date ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; send Telegram ONLY if the insert created a row (insert-then-send dedup); skip if chatId null. NO cron added. Next day available at 00:00 local is emergent: the gate keys on local dates, so when the local clock crosses midnight localToday(tz) returns a fresh empty date with 0 srcs. daily-reminder stays Bali-fixed (out of scope).
spec.rls: No RLS policy changes required. All in-scope writes and reads use createServiceSupabase() (service_role), which bypasses RLS, matching every existing route here. New functions user_tz, is_day_closed, day_progress and the rewritten RPCs are SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path=empty with REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC and GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role only (identical to existing closed-day RPCs in 20260620160000). Do NOT grant anon or authenticated. profiles.timezone falls under existing profiles RLS (own-row select); no new policy since tz is consumed only server-side inside SECURITY DEFINER functions. notifications_log keeps its existing admin-only or own policies; inserts here are service-role. is_day_closed and user_tz are SECURITY DEFINER reading profiles plus submission tables; acceptable because they are service_role-only and take an explicit p_user_id (no auth.uid() leakage path opened).
spec.ui: Mostly invisible - timezone is server-resolved. apps/web/src/app/m/profile/* - OPTIONAL read-only line Chasovoy poyas plus tz so a student sees which clock governs their day. Pure display, no editor (tz derives from city at onboarding); adding an edit control is out of scope and would need re-bucketing logic. No new screens. The day-counted feedback is a Telegram push (notify-day-closed.ts), not a new component. The MiniApp daily checklist (block-status driven) turns all 4 items green automatically once block-status uses student tz. Strings Russian via existing patterns. Telegram copy: Den zaschitan! Zavtra prodolzhaem - novyy den otkroetsya v 00:00 po tvoemu vremeni. Keep all new lib files under 200 lines.
implementationOrder: 1. Migration 20260621120000_v3_student_timezone_daygate.sql: ADD profiles.timezone (default Asia/Makassar) plus backfill from cities.timezone where city set; create user_tz(); is_day_closed(); day_progress(); rewrite user_closed_days, closed_dates_all, passed_blocks_all, and the closed-day is_block_unlocked to use per-user tz instead of AT TIME ZONE UTC; add partial unique index on notifications_log for day_counted dedup. Apply via npx supabase db push (IF NOT EXISTS / CREATE OR REPLACE only).
2. Regenerate types via mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types, then npx tsc --noEmit (regen after ADD COLUMN or Vercel build silently runs old code).
3. Add apps/web/src/lib/time/student-tz.ts (localToday, localDateBoundsUtc via Intl.DateTimeFormat).
4. Add apps/web/src/lib/daygate/notify-day-closed.ts (is_day_closed RPC plus insert-then-send dedup plus sendTelegramMessage).

5. Patch the 4 upload/mark routes (cross-photo/upload, prayer/[blockId], recitation/upload, locations/upload): select timezone plus telegram_chat_id alongside existing selects, stamp local dates, call maybeNotifyDayClosed after a successful submission. Leave test_daily_accel virtual-date branches untouched.
6. Patch block-status/[blockId]/route.ts: drop todayUTC(); compute today plus UTC bounds via student-tz, or switch the 4 today-checks to day_progress RPC. Verify closedDays output unchanged.

7. Manual verify: set a test profile timezone=America/Los_Angeles, submit all 4 around UTC midnight, confirm day closes on one local date and push arrives exactly once; re-submit gives no duplicate push; cross next local midnight gives fresh empty day; no-city profile defaults to Bali and still works.
8. Update memory note (day-model now per-student tz, not UTC) and the church-platform rule line about the day boundary.
spec.risks: - IDEMPOTENCY (push): a day flips closed once, but later same-date submissions re-run the check. Dedup on user_id, day_counted, block_id, local_date. Concurrency race (two uploads both pass an existence check) handled by partial UNIQUE INDEX plus INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING and send ONLY if the insert affected a row (insert-then-send, never send-then-insert). - NO CITY / NO TZ: profiles.timezone defaults Asia/Makassar; city_id NULL or city tz empty/UTC keeps Bali. Fine for Bali-start but a Moscow student who skipped city onboarding is 8h off. Ensure onboarding sets city before course_started_at; backfill is one-time and later city changes do NOT update profiles.timezone (by design) - provide an admin/SQL path to correct a student tz. - INVALID IANA tz: AT TIME ZONE bad raises and breaks the all-users RPCs (closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all). Only ever write tz copied from cities.timezone (already valid IANA) or the literal default; wrap with COALESCE(NULLIF(...),Asia/Makassar). No PG-side IANA validator, so rely on the controlled backfill. - RETROACTIVE RE-BUCKETING: switching RPC conversion from UTC to tz changes how stored created_at maps to dates; mid-block days can move or merge. At Bali (UTC+8) rows from 02:00-07:59 local shift forward a day (often MERGING a previously-split day, usually helps). BUT historical cross/prayer DATE columns were stamped UTC and are NOT retroactively fixed, so old cross/prayer may sit on a UTC date while recitation/location move to local, breaking the 4-src match for PAST days. Options: a one-time data migration shifting historical cross/prayer by tz offset, OR accept only forward days are tz-correct (simplest; recommend for launch). Run on a quiet window. - 5-vs-4 SRC DRIFT: _helper migrations still encode 5 srcs including trainer; authoritative live version is the 4-src one in mestopisaniya_daily (latest). Re-create ALL aggregate RPCs in the new migration with 4 srcs plus tz so an older definition cannot win on out-of-order apply. - notifications_log SHAPE COLLISION: two migrations create it differently; verify live columns (execute_sql against information_schema) before coding payload or dedup. - is_block_unlocked DUAL DEFINITION (weekly time-based in 20260618100000 vs closed-day at least 7 in 20260620160000): confirm which is live before rewriting so this change does not accidentally flip the block-unlock model.
spec.summary: Move the daily-gate day boundary from hard-coded UTC to each student local timezone, and fire a per-student push (den zaschitan, zavtra prodolzhaem) the moment a day 4 requirements first all complete. ROOT CAUSE (verified): every date is stamped UTC. Insert routes use new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) for submitted_date, prayed_date, cross dateStr, prayer markDate; recitations and location_attempts use COALESCE(effective_date,(created_at AT TIME ZONE UTC)::date). The closed-day RPCs user_closed_days, closed_dates_all, passed_blocks_all and the closed-day variant of is_block_unlocked (in 20260620160000_v3_mestopisaniya_daily.sql, the live 4-src version: cross, prayer, recv-audio, loc-video) all share this UTC assumption. block-status uses todayUTC(). A Bali student (UTC+8) acting before 08:00 local lands on the PREVIOUS UTC date; since the gate needs count(DISTINCT src)=4 on ONE date, an evening session split across UTC midnight never closes. ARCHITECTURE: one server-resolved student timezone (new profiles.timezone, backfilled from cities.timezone, fallback Asia/Makassar = Bali-start). (a) stamp DATE columns in student tz at insert. (b) in RPCs replace AT TIME ZONE UTC with student tz. (c) block-status computes today in student tz. (d) notification plus next-day-availability is EVENT-BASED not a new cron: after each successful submission the route re-checks is_day_closed(user,block,localDate); if it just flipped true, send the push once (idempotent via notifications_log). Next day available at 00:00 local needs no unlock cron because the gate keys on local dates, so a new local date is automatically an empty day. Block-to-block opening still uses the existing weekly is_block_unlocked; this only fixes day-closing within a block.

critique.verdict: REVISE BEFORE IMPLEMENTING. The core architecture (per-student timezone on profiles, Intl-based localToday, insert-then-send dedup via partial unique index, event-based next-day unlock) is sound and the UTC root-cause is correctly diagnosed and verified against live code. But the spec contains one model-breaking factual error about which is_block_unlocked is live, a retroactive re-bucketing hazard the spec itself flags but then defers in a way that will corrupt past closed-days at launch, and several mismatches with actual symbols/columns. RLS posture is acceptable (all service_role, SECURITY DEFINER with explicit p_user_id, no anon/authenticated grants, no visibility holes opened for curators/students). Fix the items below first.
corrections: CRITICAL — WRONG live is_block_unlocked: the spec's summary says 'Block-to-block opening still uses the existing weekly is_block_unlocked' and treats the weekly-vs-closed-day as merely a 'risk to confirm'. Verified against the live DB (pg_proc): the LIVE is_block_unlocked is the CLOSED-DAY '>=7 days' version from 20260620160000_v3_mestopisaniya_daily.sql (Jun 20 > Jun 18 weekly migration, so the closed-day def won the last apply). The weekly version in 20260618100000 is dead. Therefore rewriting is_block_unlocked with per-user tz (step 4) DOES change the live block-to-block unlock model — it is NOT out of scope. Either (a) explicitly own this: rewrite the closed-day is_block_unlocked with tz and re-test block unlock end-to-end, or (b) decide blocks should unlock weekly and resurrect the weekly def — but you cannot claim 'this only fixes day-closing within a block.' Also note lib/access/block-gate.ts docstring still describes the weekly model — pre-existing code/doc drift to fix in the same PR.

CRITICAL — retroactive re-bucketing will break PAST closed-days, and the recommended 'accept only forward days' option is wrong for non-Bali backfill: cross/prayer are stamped to DATE columns (UTC today), while recitation-audio and location-video are stored as created_at TIMESTAMPTZ and bucketed via (created_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date in the RPC. After the switch, for ANY already-active non-Bali student the audio/video sources shift to local dates while the historical cross/prayer DATE rows stay on their old UTC date — so count(DISTINCT src)=4 silently fails for past days and previously-closed days un-close, which can REVOKE a block that was unlocked. The spec's own risk note says this but then 'recommends forward-only for launch' — that is only safe if NO non-Bali student has historical rows. Required: before deciding, run a query for distinct profiles.timezone among students with existing cross/prayer/recitation/location rows; if all are effectively Bali, forward-only is fine; otherwise you MUST include the one-time historical cross/prayer date-shift migration (or freeze evaluation of already-closed days). Do not defer this to a post-launch fixup.

CRITICAL — profiles.timezone DEFAULT 'Asia/Makassar' contradicts cities.timezone DEFAULT 'UTC' and breaks the backfill predicate as written: verified live, cities.timezone is NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UTC' and profiles has NO timezone column yet. The spec backfills 'WHERE c.timezone NOT IN (empty, UTC)'. So every city currently defaulting to 'UTC' (must verify which cities actually carry real IANA values vs the inherited 'UTC' default) will be SKIPPED and those students silently get Asia/Makassar. Run SELECT DISTINCT timezone FROM cities first; if active non-Bali cities still hold 'UTC', the backfill leaves real Moscow/LA students 8-11h off. Either fix cities.timezone data first, or make the skip-list explicit and provide the admin SQL correction path the spec promises (the spec mentions it but defines no endpoint/skill — at minimum document the exact UPDATE).

is_day_closed predicate is incomplete vs the live 4-src rule for the location source: the live RPCs require count(DISTINCT location_id) >= rc.req where rc.req = count of is_required locations FOR THAT BLOCK (block_locations_to_recite WHERE is_required, grouped by block_id). The spec's is_day_closed prose says 'DISTINCT passed video_note locations at least the required count' but must JOIN block_locations_to_recite on l.block_id = p_block_id AND l.is_required and compare to the per-block required count, AND must respect COALESCE(effective_date, (created_at AT TIME ZONE tz)::date) for accel rows. If is_day_closed omits the per-block required-count comparison or the effective_date COALESCE, it will disagree with user_closed_days and either fire the push early or never. Make is_day_closed literally reuse the same 4-branch logic (now centralized) so day_progress, user_closed_days, closed_dates_all, passed_blocks_all and is_block_unlocked all share ONE definition.

accel / test_daily_accel branch interacts badly with the new event-notify and tz stamping — spec says 'leave accel untouched' but doesn't: in cross-photo and prayer the accel branch stamps a VIRTUAL date (anchor 2000-01-01 + offset), NOT localToday(tz). maybeNotifyDayClosed is called 'after upsert' with localDate — if you pass localToday(tz) for an accel user, is_day_closed(user,block,localToday) will be FALSE (their data is on 2000-01-XX), so no push (probably fine), but if you pass the virtual date you'd spam pushes per virtual day. Decide explicitly: skip maybeNotifyDayClosed entirely when test_daily_accel is true. Also recitation/locations accel uses effective_date (anchor 2000-01-01) — is_day_closed must use the SAME COALESCE(effective_date,...) or accel test days never close under the new predicate.

notifications_log SHAPE — verified, only one matters: live table is the STUDENT shape (user_id, channel, type, payload jsonb, status, error_message, created_at). The curator-log migration 20260521200100 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS was a NO-OP (table already existed). So the dedup index on (user_id, (payload->>'block_id'), (payload->>'local_date')) WHERE type='day_counted' is valid. Two concrete fixes: (1) channel is NOT NULL with a CHECK channel IN ('telegram','email') — your INSERT must set channel='telegram' or it violates the constraint; the spec's notify-day-closed payload shape omits channel. (2) For the partial unique index use explicit text expressions (payload->>'block_id'), (payload->>'local_date') and ensure both are always present and stringified identically on every insert, else ON CONFLICT won't match. Confirm the index expression list exactly matches the INSERT's conflict target.

block-status route created_at bounds have a pre-existing off-by-epsilon and must be replaced, not patched: current code filters recitation/location by .gte(today T00:00:00.000Z).lt(today T23:59:59.999Z) — that's a ~1ms gap at end of day AND it's UTC. The spec's preferred option (switch the 4 today-checks to a day_progress RPC) is the right call — adopt it rather than computing localDateBoundsUtc in TS, to avoid re-deriving the bounds bug in local tz. If you do keep TS bounds, use [gteUtc, ltUtc) where ltUtc = next-local-midnight in UTC (half-open), not 23:59:59.999.

Route path/symbol accuracy: there is no module-level todayUTC in cross-photo (it's todayDateStr) and block-status has its own todayUTC; the trainer route apps/web/src/app/api/m/trainer/[blockId]/route.ts ALSO defines todayUTC and was historically a gate source (the 5-src 'trainer' the spec warns about). Confirm trainer is fully dropped from the live gate (live RPCs show 4 src, no trainer — good) but the trainer route still stamps student_block_daily_trainer in UTC; if anything still reads it per-day, include it or explicitly document it's display-only. Also cross-photo embeds dateStr into the Storage path ({user}/{block}/{date}.ext) — switching to local date changes future storage paths (benign, but note it so old vs new photos for the same calendar day don't collide/confuse).

Storage-path / DATE-stamp consistency for cross & prayer: spec says cross/prayer DATE columns become local-stamped 'after the insert-route fix so need no conversion.' Ensure the UPSERT ON CONFLICT (user_id, block_id, submitted_date/prayed_date) still dedups correctly the day the cutover happens — a student who already submitted under a UTC date and re-submits under the new local date on the same wall-clock day will create a SECOND row for what is arguably the same day. Low impact but verify the unique constraint columns and that this doesn't double-count toward closed-days during the transition window.

Add an explicit IANA-validity guard for profiles.timezone: there is no Postgres-side IANA validator and AT TIME ZONE <bad> RAISES, which would break the all-users scans (closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all) for EVERY user, not just the bad one. The spec relies on 'only ever write tz copied from cities.timezone.' Strengthen: (a) keep the COALESCE(NULLIF(timezone,''),'Asia/Makassar') everywhere as specified, and (b) add a CHECK or a trigger validating the value via a now()-cast test, OR constrain writes so the only paths that set profiles.timezone are the controlled backfill and an admin-only function. Otherwise one malformed admin SQL edit takes down the whole tracking leaderboard.

Out-of-order / re-apply safety: the spec correctly insists on re-creating ALL aggregate RPCs with CREATE OR REPLACE in the new migration so an older 5-src def can't win. Good — but also add the same REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC / REVOKE FROM anon, authenticated / GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role block for user_tz, is_day_closed, day_progress AND the re-created aggregates (the live 20260620160000 versions rely on inherited grants; re-creating with CREATE OR REPLACE preserves grants, but new functions need them set explicitly). Confirm search_path = '' (empty string) on all, matching the live functions, and fully-qualify every table as public.* (the spec says SET search_path=empty — ensure it's the empty-string form Postgres expects, not a literal 'empty').

specs.title: Rich student profile (occupation/bio/testimony/media/links) + public profile + per-user feed view

spec.summary:
Add a rich student profile (occupation, bio, conversion testimony, links, attachable media) plus a PUBLIC per-user profile page at /m/u/[id] reachable from the tracking leaderboard, showing that user's own community/emotions posts (community_posts filtered by author_id). The codebase already has every pattern needed: service-role + Telegram initData auth (resolveUserId), a public `avatars` bucket and a private `community-media` bucket with signed URLs, a feed query that joins community_posts→profiles, a tracking route, and a JSON profile-update route. No Supabase user session exists in /m/*, so RLS on the new objects stays "deny to anon/authenticated" and all access goes through service-role API routes (same model as community_posts). Effort: M.

KEY GOTCHA: the tracking API (apps/web/src/app/api/m/tracking/route.ts line 219) deliberately strips `id` before returning rows, and LeaderRow (leaderboard.types.ts) has no `id`. Cards are therefore NOT linkable today. Step 1 of the UI work is re-adding `id` to LeaderRow + the ranked output so LeaderboardCard can link to /m/u/[id].

XSS: all new fields are STUDENT input → render only via React JSX (auto-escaped) or textContent. Never dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Links: validate http(s) scheme server-side and render with rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" + target="_blank"; reject javascript:/data: schemes.
spec.apiRoutes:
Все роуты: POST, body содержит initData, auth через resolveUserId (lib/telegram/resolve-user.ts), клиент через createServiceSupabase. Паттерн ошибок { error: { code, message } } как в существующих.

1) GET-данные собственного профиля для редактора — РАСШИРИТЬ существующий, не плодить:
   POST /api/m/profile  (или /api/miniapp/profile — AvatarUpload уже бьёт сюда; проверить какой реально существует и дополнить его SELECT новыми полями occupation,bio,testimony,profile_links + avatar_url). Возвращает { occupation,bio,testimony,profile_links,avatar_url }.

2) POST /api/m/profile/update  (НОВЫЙ, ~120 строк) — сохранение текстовых полей.
   Body: { initData, occupation?, bio?, testimony?, profile_links? }.
   Валидация (по образцу update-location/route.ts): trim + length-clamp (occupation 160, bio 1000, testimony 4000); profile_links — массив <=5, каждый {label<=60, url}; url проходит new URL() и protocol ∈ {http:,https:} иначе reject BAD_LINK. UPDATE profiles ... WHERE id=auth.userId. Ответ { ok:true }.

3) POST /api/m/profile/media (НОВЫЙ, multipart, ~140 строк) — загрузка медиа-файла. По образцу api/m/community/post/route.ts:
   form { initData, kind, file, caption? }. Валидация mime/size (50MB как community-media). storagePath = `${userId}/${randomUUID()}.${ext}`, upload в bucket profile-media upsert:false. INSERT profile_media. Лимит: <=12 файлов/пользователь (SELECT count перед insert). Ответ { ok:true, item:{id,kind,media_url(signed),caption} }.

4) POST /api/m/profile/media/delete (НОВЫЙ, JSON, ~60 строк) — { initData, media_id }. Проверка user_id===auth.userId, затем storage.remove + DELETE row. Ответ { ok:true }.

5) POST /api/m/u (НОВЫЙ, ~150 строк) — ПУБЛИЧНЫЙ профиль другого ученика.
   Body: { initData, target_id }. auth.userId = зритель. 
   - SELECT target profile: id,full_name,contact_info,avatar_path,role,occupation,bio,testimony,profile_links, cities(name_ru), hidden_from_tracking.
   - Видимость: если target.hidden_from_tracking=TRUE и target_id!==auth.userId и зритель не привилегирован (role admin/super_admin/curator) → 404 (как «нет такого профиля»). Это согласуется с тем, что трекинг уже фильтрует hidden_from_tracking.
   - avatar_url через публичный bucket avatars (как в tracking/route.ts).
   - posts: SELECT community_posts WHERE author_id=target_id AND is_deleted=false ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20; signed URLs пачкой из community-media (createSignedUrls, как в feed/route.ts). can_delete = (author===viewer) || privileged.
   - profile_media: SELECT по target_id ORDER BY sort_order; signed URLs из profile-media.
   Ответ { profile:{...}, media:[...], posts:[...] }.

NB: НЕ создавать клиентский Supabase-доступ — всё через service_role server-side (правило security.md + community-модель).
spec.rls:
Модель /m/* = НЕТ supabase auth-сессии у клиента, весь доступ идёт через service_role в API после проверки Telegram initData. Поэтому RLS — это «забор», а не основной механизм авторизации (так уже сделано для community_posts).

- public.profiles: RLS уже включена. Новые колонки наследуют существующие политики — НИЧЕГО не открываем для anon. Чтение чужого профиля происходит ТОЛЬКО через POST /api/m/u (service_role) с проверкой видимости в коде.
- public.profile_media: ENABLE RLS; никаких политик для anon; authenticated SELECT USING(true) — это задел на будущую миграцию на supabase-сессии, фактический контроль — в API (user_id===auth.userId на write/delete).
- storage bucket profile-media: PRIVATE (public=false). Раздача исключительно через createSignedUrl/createSignedUrls в server-роутах (TTL 3600), как community-media. НИКОГДА не строить публичный /object/public/ URL для него.
- storage bucket avatars: остаётся PUBLIC (уже так) — аватар на публичном профиле = тот же публичный URL.

Логика видимости публичного профиля (в API /api/m/u, не в SQL-функции is_visible_to — её для /m/* не используют, т.к. нет auth.uid()):
  viewer видит target если: target===viewer ИЛИ target.hidden_from_tracking=false ИЛИ viewer.role ∈ {admin,super_admin,curator}.
Это ровно зеркалит фильтр трекинга (.eq('hidden_from_tracking',false)) — нельзя кликнуть на того, кого не видно в списке. Привилегированные роли видят скрытых (как webhook isOwner видит hidden).

Запись/удаление media и текстовых полей — строго `WHERE id/user_id = auth.userId`, target_id игнорируется на write-путях. is_protected тут не релевантен (не меняем роли).
spec.ui:

Состояния loading/404/empty как в TrackingClient/CommunityClient.
- apps/web/src/app/m/u/[id]/public-profile.css.

Все строки — на русском. Никаких alert/confirm — inline-подтверждение удаления как в PostCard (двухтап «Удалить?»).
implementationOrder: 1. Миграция A (profiles: occupation/bio/testimony/profile_links) + Миграция B (profile_media + bucket profile-media). Применить через mcp__supabase apply_migration или supabase db push.
2. Регенерировать types.ts (mcp__supabase generate_typescript_types) + npm run build/tsc --noEmit — иначе Vercel молча соберёт старое (memory: feedback_regen_types_after_migration).

3. Backend: расширить существующий GET-профиль (/api/m/profile или /api/miniapp/profile) новыми полями; POST /api/m/profile/update; POST /api/m/profile/media (multipart) + /media/delete. Тест curl с DEV_BYPASS_USER_ID.
4. Backend: POST /api/m/u — публичный профиль с проверкой видимости (hidden_from_tracking + привилегии) + posts(community_posts by author_id) + signed URLs.
5. Frontend редактор: ProfileFields, ProfileLinksEditor, ProfileMedia; подключить в profile/page.tsx; profile.css.
6. Frontend публичная страница: /m/u/[id]/page.tsx + PublicProfileClient (переиспользовать PostCard, Avatar); public-profile.css.
7. Сделать карточки трекинга кликабельными: добавить id в LeaderRow, перестать стрипить id в tracking/route.ts, обернуть LeaderboardCard в Link на /m/u/[id].
8. Прогон: build + lint (<200 строк/файл), ручная проверка XSS (вставить <script>/javascript: в bio/url), проверка что скрытые профили дают 404 для обычного ученика.
9. (Опц.) Поиск по ученикам в трекинге — ОТЛОЖИТЬ как отдельную фичу: текущий трекинг отдаёт полный список, поиск решается клиентским фильтром по name; глобальный server-side поиск — вне объёма.
spec.risks:
- XSS (главный): occupation/bio/testimony/caption/link.label — ввод ученика. Рендерить ТОЛЬКО через JSX (React auto-escape) или textContent; запрет dangerouslySetInnerHTML. URL ссылок: server-side new URL() + protocol∈{http,https}, иначе reject; на рендере target=_blank rel=\"noopener noreferrer nofollow\". Reject javascript:/data:/vbscript:.
- Кликабельность трекинга СЛОМАНА by design: id стрипается в tracking/route.ts:219 и отсутствует в LeaderRow — без шага 7 ссылки невозможны. Легко упустить.
- Утечка приватных медиа: profile-media ДОЛЖЕН быть private + signed URL; ошибочно публичный бакет = доступ по прямому URL. avatars наоборот публичный — не путать схемы URL.
- Видимость: если /api/m/u не повторит фильтр hidden_from_tracking, можно открыть скрытых тестировщиков/админов через прямой /m/u/<uuid>. Нужна проверка зеркально трекингу + 404 (не 403, чтобы не палить существование).
- Перебор размера файла: bucket лимит 50MB + проверка в API; добавить лимит кол-ва (<=12) чтобы не забить storage.
- Лимит 200 строк/файл: profile/page.tsx уже ~58, но с новыми секциями вынести в подкомпоненты; /api/m/u близко к 150 — держать тонким, signed URLs пачкой.
- Cache аватара/медиа: при перезаливке avatar путь стабилен (userId.ext) → AvatarUpload уже добавляет ?t=Date.now(); media — uuid-путь, проблемы нет.
- Types drift: пропуск регенерации types.ts → build падает молча на Vercel (известная боль проекта).
- i18n: только русские строки, никакого хардкода EN.

critique.verdict: APPROVE WITH REQUIRED CHANGES. The spec is well-grounded in the real codebase (resolve-user, service-role model, community-media signed-URL pattern, the tracking id-strip gotcha at route.ts ~line 219, the two profile route locations) and its security posture is sound. But there are ~7 concrete correctness gaps that will cause build/runtime/data bugs if implemented as written. Fix them before coding. Most severe: migration A is NOT idempotent (ADD CONSTRAINT has no IF NOT EXISTS in Postgres), and the profile_media.kind values + the "emotions" feed claim do not match the actual schema/components the spec says to reuse.
corrections: MIGRATION SAFETY (blocker): Migration A uses `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT profiles_occupation_len CHECK(...) NOT VALID` three times. Postgres has NO `ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS`, so re-running the migration (or a second deploy that re-applies it) errors with `constraint already exists` and aborts the whole file. This breaks the project's mandatory idempotency rule. Wrap each in a guard: `DO $$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='profiles_occupation_len') THEN ALTER TABLE public.profiles ADD CONSTRAINT profiles_occupation_len CHECK (occupation IS NULL OR char_length(occupation)<=160) NOT VALID; END IF; END $$;` (same for bio/testimony). Or drop the DB constraints entirely and rely on API length-clamping (the spec already clamps in /api/m/profile/update), which is simpler and matches how community_posts/emotions enforce limits today.

SCHEMA/COMPONENT MISMATCH (blocker for reuse): profile_media.kind is specced as IN ('photo','audio','video','document'), but the actual community_posts.kind and FeedPost PostKind are ('text','audio','video_note','photo') and PostCard.tsx renders ONLY text/audio/video_note/photo. The public profile is specced to REUSE PostCard. Result: 'video' and 'document' profile media will not render (PostCard has no branch for them), and there is no PDF/document viewer anywhere in the codebase. Either (a) restrict profile_media.kind to align with what the UI can show ('photo','audio','video_note') and drop 'document' until a viewer exists, or (b) accept that the public-profile media gallery needs its OWN render component (not PostCard) — the spec must say which. Also note the bucket allowed_mime_types includes application/pdf with no consumer.

FEATURE CLAIM WRONG (emotions): The summary says the public profile shows the user's 'community/emotions posts (community_posts filtered by author_id)'. Emotions are NOT in community_posts — they live in a separate table public.student_block_emotions (kind IN text/audio/video_note, per migration 20260524120000). Filtering community_posts by author_id will return ONLY global-feed posts, never the student's emotions/testimonies. If emotions are meant to appear on the public profile, the /api/m/u route must also query student_block_emotions (and sign its media from whatever bucket it uses) — that is extra scope not in the data model. Recommend: drop the word 'emotions' from the claim, OR explicitly add a student_block_emotions SELECT. Note the RLS on student_block_emotions is `auth.uid()=user_id` (own-only), so exposing another user's emotions on a public profile is a deliberate visibility decision that should be called out, not implicit.

VISIBILITY — CURATOR LEAK RISK (verify): The spec grants viewers with role IN {admin,super_admin,curator} the right to see ANY hidden profile via /api/m/u. For admin/super_admin this mirrors the webhook isOwner behavior and is fine. But a CURATOR seeing every hidden tester/admin profile is broader than the project's curator visibility rule (CLAUDE.md: curator sees only their own group + curators of their city). hidden_from_tracking is set TRUE for admins/super_admins and tester @omeleshinka, so this specifically lets any curator open admin/tester profiles by guessing/using a UUID. Tighten: either (a) restrict the hidden-bypass to admin/super_admin only (curators get 404 on hidden targets, matching the tracking list they actually see), or (b) explicitly document that curator-sees-all-hidden is an accepted relaxation for this internal feature. As written it contradicts the stated curator visibility boundary.

AUTH PARITY GAP: resolveUserId returns 403 WAITLIST for any non-privileged, non-whitelisted user. Every new /m/* route (profile/update, profile/media, profile/media/delete, /api/m/u) inherits this correctly. Good — but confirm the public profile viewer being non-privileged still passes (it must be whitelisted to even be in /m/*). No change needed; just verify /api/m/u doesn't accidentally require the VIEWER to be privileged.

WRONG ROUTE TARGET for the GET-profile extension: The spec says extend '/api/m/profile (или /api/miniapp/profile — проверить какой реально существует)'. Verified: there is NO /api/m/profile route.ts (only /api/m/profile/update-location/). The avatar/onboarding profile reader that AvatarUpload and LocationSettings actually call is POST /api/miniapp/profile (apps/web/src/app/api/miniapp/profile/route.ts), which currently returns onboarding_done/country_id/city_id/curator_id/full_name/avatar_url. So the new fields (occupation,bio,testimony,profile_links) must be added to /api/miniapp/profile SELECT and response — not to a nonexistent /api/m/profile. The spec's hedge should be resolved to the concrete file before coding.

AVATAR URL pattern is consistent (no change, but note): tracking/route.ts, feed/route.ts and miniapp/profile all build the public avatars URL as `${SUPABASE_URL}/storage/v1/object/public/avatars/${path}`. The public-profile route must use this SAME public pattern for avatar (not signed), while profile-media/community-media use createSignedUrls. The spec says this correctly — just ensure /api/m/u doesn't accidentally try to sign an avatars path (avatars is a public bucket; signing a path in a public bucket via createSignedUrl still works but is wasteful and inconsistent).

TIMEZONE (out of scope but flag): The spec leaves the known UTC-vs-Bali day-boundary bug in block-status/route.ts (todayUTC, cities.timezone unused) untouched. This feature doesn't introduce new date logic, so no regression — but profile/media created_at and any 'today' display should stay UTC-consistent with the rest of the app; do not introduce a NEW timezone convention here. Recommend explicitly stating 'no date-boundary logic in this feature' to avoid a dev half-fixing it.

FILE LIMIT precision: page.tsx is 58 lines (spec says ~58, correct) and the plan to extract ProfileFields/ProfileLinksEditor/ProfileMedia is right. /api/m/u is specced at ~150 lines doing target SELECT + visibility + posts + signedUrls + profile_media + signedUrls — realistically this exceeds 200 lines once error handling matches the existing verbose try/catch style (see update-location at ~120 lines for a far simpler op). Plan to split media-signing into a small lib helper (e.g. lib/storage/signed.ts) shared with feed/route.ts to stay under 200.

MINOR — profile_links JSONB validation: storing arbitrary JSONB with only API-side validation means a future direct INSERT or a missed clamp could persist a javascript: URL. Since the column is NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', also consider a lightweight CHECK that jsonb_typeof(profile_links)='array' to prevent shape corruption, and ALWAYS re-validate url scheme on the READ/render path of /api/m/u (defense in depth), not only on write — the spec validates on write but the public route must also enforce rel=noopener noreferrer nofollow + scheme check when emitting links, in case legacy rows exist.

specs.title: Pink theme for girls (gender) + UI optimization

spec.summary: Pink theme for female students, built ON TOP of the existing data-theme system (NOT from scratch). The app already has a full theme engine: `Theme = 'light'|'dark'|'stars'` in ThemeProvider.tsx, `data-theme` attr on <html>, localStorage key `krest-theme`, anti-flash inline script, `applyTelegramTheme()` palettes in lib/telegram/theme.ts, and CSS keyed off `[data-theme="..."]`. The whole app's accent comes from two CSS vars `--accent-solid` / `--accent-gradient` (globals.css lines 29-32) read by ~17 CSS files. So a pink theme is mainly: (a) add `'pink'` to the Theme union, (b) override `--accent-solid/--accent-gradient` + soft pink surfaces under `[data-theme="pink"]`, (c) add a `pink` palette to lib/telegram/theme.ts, (d) persist a per-user gender + theme preference on profiles so pink auto-applies for girls on every device. Two product decisions to confirm with Михаил (flagged in risks): does pink AUTO-apply from gender='female', or is it a free manual choice? I spec the recommended hybrid: gender seeds the default, user can still toggle. Effort M. Migration adds two columns (`gender`, `theme_pref`) with IF NOT EXISTS, no new RLS needed (all /m writes go through service-role API routes that bypass RLS; profiles has no client UPDATE policy). NOTE: this feature is non-doctrinal cosmetic — gender is collected ONLY for theming, keep it optional/nullable to avoid forcing a binary choice in a church context.
spec.apiRoutes:
All /m writes already go through service-role API routes (createServiceSupabase, auth via resolveUserId(initData)). Mirror that exactly.

1) CHANGE app/api/miniapp/profile/route.ts (POST, auth: Telegram initData):
   - Add `gender, theme_pref` to the .select() list and to the JSON response, so client can seed theme on load (today it selects onboarding_done,country_id,city_id,curator_id,full_name,avatar_path).

2) ADD app/api/m/profile/update-theme/route.ts (POST, auth: Telegram initData via resolveUserId) — model on existing update-location/route.ts (<200 lines):
   - Body: { initData, theme_pref?: 'light'|'dark'|'stars'|'pink', gender?: 'male'|'female' }
   - Validate theme_pref ∈ allowed set and gender ∈ {male,female} (else 400 BAD_REQUEST, same error envelope { error:{ code,message } } used project-wide).
   - createServiceSupabase().from('profiles').update({...only provided fields...}).eq('id', auth.userId). Returns { ok: true }.
   - Service-role bypasses RLS; route only ever writes auth.userId's own row → no privilege escalation.

3) CHANGE app/api/miniapp/onboarding/route.ts (POST) — add optional `gender` to body type and to the `update` object (only set if provided, same conditional pattern already used for curator_id/full_name). Lets onboarding seed gender→default pink without a second request.

No new admin/panel endpoints required for MVP. (Optional later: surface gender as a badge in panel/(dash)/students — not in scope.)
spec.rls:
NO new RLS policies needed, and this is the key codebase-specific point:
- profiles has SELECT policy `profiles_select_visible` (is_visible_to) but NO client-side UPDATE/INSERT policy — all student profile writes happen server-side via createServiceSupabase() (service-role), which bypasses RLS entirely. update-location and onboarding already write profiles this way with zero RLS policy.
- The new update-theme route uses the identical service-role path and only ever targets `.eq('id', auth.userId)` (the initData-resolved caller), so a user can only change their own gender/theme. No cross-user write surface is opened.
- gender/theme_pref are non-sensitive cosmetic fields; they ride along the existing `profiles_select_visible` SELECT policy with no change. Do NOT add a permissive client UPDATE policy on profiles (would widen attack surface beyond these two fields). Keep the service-role-only write discipline (per .claude/rules/security.md + database.md).
- Confirm with mcp__supabase get_advisors after migration that no new RLS advisory is raised on profiles.
spec.ui:
CSS-vars approach (non-breaking, additive — light theme untouched), matching the existing dark-theme pattern.

ADD apps/web/src/app/m/theme-pink.css (import in app/m/layout.tsx after theme-dark.css). Scope EVERYTHING under [data-theme="pink"]:
  - Override the two driving vars so all ~17 accent-consuming CSS files turn pink automatically:
      [data-theme="pink"] { --accent-from:#F472B6; --accent-to:#EC4899; --accent-gradient:linear-gradient(135deg,#F9A8D4,#EC4899); --accent-solid:#EC4899; }
  - Soft pink surfaces (override miniapp.css hardcoded #eef0f3 bg + keep dark text for contrast/WCAG):
      [data-theme="pink"] .miniapp-root { background:#fdf2f8; color:#16181d; --surface:#ffffff; --surface-border:rgba(236,72,153,0.14); --hairline:rgba(236,72,153,0.12); --section-title:#16181d; --track:rgba(236,72,153,0.12); }
  - onb-cta stays dark pill (pink is a LIGHT-family theme); optionally tint with pink border. Most components need ZERO change because they read --accent-solid/--accent-gradient.
  - This is far less work than theme-dark.css because pink reuses the light layout/contrast and only re-tints the accent.

CHANGE components/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx:
  - `export type Theme = 'light'|'dark'|'stars'|'pink'`.
  - Anti-flash script: accept 'pink' from localStorage; default still 'light'.
  - On mount: after reading data-theme/localStorage, fetch /api/miniapp/profile; if theme_pref set → setTheme(theme_pref); else if gender==='female' && no explicit choice → setTheme('pink'). Persist explicit user toggles to localStorage AND POST update-theme so it follows the user across devices.
  - setTheme: when user explicitly toggles, also POST /api/m/profile/update-theme {theme_pref}. Keep DECISION: pink should NOT be in the floating-toggle cycle (avoid a man accidentally cycling into pink); expose pink only as a deliberate row choice in profile (see below). So leave THEME_CYCLE = ['light','dark','stars'] for the FAB.

CHANGE components/theme/ThemeToggle.tsx: add 'pink' to NEXT_LABEL/CURRENT_LABEL maps + a heart/flower ThemeIcon case (so the row variant renders correctly when theme is pink). Floating FAB cycle unchanged.

CHANGE lib/telegram/theme.ts: add `pink` to local Theme type + PALETTES with a light-family pink palette (--tg-bg:#FDF2F8, --tg-link:#EC4899, --tg-button:#EC4899/#16181d, secondary/section bg #fff, separators pink-tinted). resolveTheme: treat 'pink' as itself. This paints Telegram native header/bg pink too.

ADD a theme/gender control in app/m/profile/page.tsx + new component app/m/profile/ThemeSettings.tsx (<200 lines, 'use client'):
  - New "Оформление" pf-card with explicit options: Светлая / Тёмная / Звёзды / Розовая (girls) — segmented or row list; calls useTheme().setTheme + persists. This is where pink is intentionally selectable.
  - Optional "Пол" row (Не указан / Девушка / Парень) → POST update-theme {gender}; selecting Девушка offers to switch to pink via toast.

OPTIONAL onboarding: add a tiny gender step OR a checkbox on NameInput.tsx ("Девушка — включить розовое оформление") that passes gender into the existing onboarding POST. Keep optional/skippable.

Strings all Russian (per rules). Files all <200 lines. Server Components default; theme files already client.
implementationOrder: 1. Migration: write supabase/migrations/20260621120000_v3_gender_theme_pref.sql (gender + theme_pref columns, CHECK constraints via DO/EXCEPTION, COMMENTs). Apply with /run-migration. Then regenerate types.ts + run tsc --noEmit (per memory note — Vercel build fails silently otherwise).
2. lib/telegram/theme.ts: add 'pink' to Theme type, add PINK palette to PALETTES, handle 'pink' in resolveTheme. Verify no TS errors.

3. components/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx: extend Theme union to include 'pink'; update anti-flash script + useEffect resolver to accept 'pink'; add profile-fetch effect that seeds theme from theme_pref / gender; setTheme persists to localStorage + POST update-theme. Keep FAB cycle = light/dark/stars only.
4. components/theme/ThemeToggle.tsx: add 'pink' label entries + ThemeIcon case (heart/flower). Floating cycle unchanged.
5. Create app/m/theme-pink.css (accent var override + soft pink surfaces, all scoped to [data-theme="pink"]). Import it in app/m/layout.tsx after theme-dark.css.

6. API: change app/api/miniapp/profile/route.ts to return gender+theme_pref. Add app/api/m/profile/update-theme/route.ts (service-role, initData auth, validates fields, writes own row). Change app/api/miniapp/onboarding/route.ts to accept optional gender.
7. UI: add app/m/profile/ThemeSettings.tsx (explicit theme + gender control) and wire into app/m/profile/page.tsx. Optionally add gender checkbox to onboarding NameInput.tsx.

8. Manual QA on iPhone Telegram + browser: pink applies, persists across reload/devices, native Telegram header turns pink, contrast OK, light/dark/stars unaffected, man cannot accidentally cycle into pink via FAB. Run /run-qa-review on changed files.
9. CONFIRM with Михаил the product decision (auto-from-gender vs manual) BEFORE merge — see risks. Then /deploy.
spec.risks:
PRODUCT (flag to Михаил before coding finalizes): (a) Auto-apply pink purely from gender='female' can feel presumptuous/forced; recommended hybrid = gender seeds default but theme_pref (explicit choice) always wins, and pink is intentionally selectable in profile. (b) Collecting `gender` in a church discipleship context — keep it OPTIONAL/nullable, framed strictly as 'оформление', never gate course logic on it. (c) Per CLAUDE.md, conflicts between code and SPEC.md/UI_UX_BRIEF must be reported, not silently coded — UI brief is light-theme-first; adding a girls theme is a brief deviation worth a one-line confirm.

TECHNICAL: (1) Anti-flash: theme_pref lives in DB, not localStorage, so first paint can flash light before profile fetch resolves pink → on first run after onboarding write theme_pref into localStorage immediately so the inline script picks it up next load. (2) localStorage vs DB conflict: define precedence (DB theme_pref > localStorage > gender-default > light) and write DB choice back to localStorage to converge. (3) Telegram WebView may block localStorage (already handled with try/catch in ThemeProvider) — DB seed covers it. (4) Don't add pink to the FAB toggle cycle (men cycling into pink). (5) After migration regenerate types.ts or Vercel build silently ships stale code (known project failure mode). (6) get_advisors after migration to confirm no RLS advisory. (7) theme-pink.css must come AFTER theme-dark.css in import order and be fully scoped to [data-theme=\"pink\"] so it never leaks into light/dark/stars. (8) Contrast: pink accent on white must keep WCAG AA for text/buttons — use #EC4899 (passes on white for large/bold) and keep body text #16181d, not pink.

corrections: Overall: spec is well grounded in real code. Theme engine, service-role write discipline, and profiles RLS (only a SELECT policy profiles_select_visible, no client UPDATE or INSERT) all match. Migration is additive and nullable, no data loss. No cross-user write or visibility hole. Fix the items below first.

BLOCKER migration timestamp out of order. Proposed 20260621120000 at 12:00 is earlier than the already applied 20260621210000_v3_daily_reminder_20.sql at 21:00 the same day. Supabase db push orders files lexicographically, so a back dated file can be skipped or corrupt migration history. Rename to later than 21:00, for example 20260621220000_v3_gender_theme_pref.sql.

CHECK constraint idempotency. The DO EXCEPTION block only catches duplicate_object, so it is not idempotent if profiles_gender_chk or profiles_theme_pref_chk already exist with a different definition. Confirm those names are unused. Validation cost is trivial because the columns are brand new and all NULL.

TypeScript compile break. In ThemeToggle the maps NEXT_LABEL and CURRENT_LABEL are typed Record of Theme to string, so extending the Theme union with pink fails tsc until pink entries plus a ThemeIcon case are added. Also the row variant of ThemeToggle is dead code today, it is not mounted anywhere, only the floating FAB is used in the m layout.

Stale claim. The profile page does not fetch the profile API. It is a pure client component using only useTelegram, avatar initialUrl hardcoded null. The theme seeding fetch must be added inside ThemeProvider, which has no fetch effect today, so there is no existing page fetch to piggyback on.

Anti flash gap. themeNoFlashScript hardcodes a check that keeps only dark or stars and otherwise falls back to light, and the mount resolver at ThemeProvider line 35 does the same coercion. Both drop a stored pink value to light. Both literal checks must add pink or the theme silently downgrades on every reload.

The telegram theme helper has its own local Theme type and its own resolveTheme with the same drop to light coercion, and PALETTES is typed Record of Theme so it will not compile without a pink entry. The spec mentions the palette but not resolveTheme. Without editing resolveTheme, applyTelegramTheme repaints pink as light.

Verified the dark theme stylesheet does not override the accent variables. It keeps the purple accent and only re tints surfaces. So pink is the first theme to override accent-solid and accent-gradient, which validates the auto pink across files claim. Note accent-from and accent-to are read by only one file, the lesson stylesheet, while accent-solid and accent-gradient are broadly used. Overriding all four as specced is correct and safe.

Timezone out of scope, no regression. The block status route computes today in pure UTC and ignores the cities timezone Bali column, which exists but is unused. This feature does not touch the day or closed day model, so it adds no timezone bug and is correctly left alone. Keep gender strictly cosmetic, never on a course logic column.

RLS invariant to state explicitly. is_visible_to limits a student to the same curator group plus completed course peers, and no client query selects all columns from profiles, client reads are narrow such as role, city_id, can_skip_block_lock. Return gender and theme_pref only from the own row profile route, authorized by resolveUserId and filtered to the caller id. Never add these fields to any feed, tracking, or community SELECT, or same group students could read each other gender.

The update theme route should validate theme_pref and gender against the same allowed sets as the database CHECK, light dark stars pink and male female, returning a 400 BAD_REQUEST with the project error envelope, so a bad value is a clean 400 not a database constraint 500. The auth pattern matching update-location is correct and opens no escalation.

Make types regeneration a hard gate. After the migration run type generation plus tsc noEmit before editing routes. The any casts on the supabase client hide column typos, and typed panel queries elsewhere break the Vercel build silently on a stale types file, a known project failure mode.

Project rules require reporting spec conflicts. The UI brief is light theme first and tech premium with no church style ornamentation, so a girls only pink theme is a brief deviation to confirm with Mikhail. Also confirm before merge whether pink auto applies from gender or is a manual choice, and whether collecting gender at all is acceptable in a church context. Keep gender nullable, optional, skippable, framed strictly as appearance, never gating course logic.

specs.title: Performance audit — make the app faster

spec.summary: Performance audit of КРЕСТ (Next.js 16 + Supabase). I read the real code. Three concrete classes of waste, in priority order: (1) DATABASE — hottest path. closed_dates_all() and passed_blocks_all() (supabase/migrations/20260620160000_v3_mestopisaniya_daily.sql and 20260618200000_v3_passed_blocks_helper.sql) UNION-ALL-scan FIVE submission tables (daily_cross, daily_prayer, recitations, location_attempts+block_locations_to_recite, daily_trainer) for ALL users, with NO date/user filter, on EVERY call. Called on panel overview (app/panel/(dash)/page.tsx via api/panel/stats/stats-data.ts), panel students (api/panel/students/route.ts), and per-student MiniApp tracking (api/m/tracking/route.ts). closed_dates_all returns one row per (user,closed-day) re-aggregated in JS. None of the daily tables have a composite (user_id,block_id,date) index, so the GROUP BYs hit sequential scans. Two near-identical RPCs (4-src vs 5-src) compound it. (2) MiniApp first paint — dashboard renders an empty client shell then fires TWO sequential round-trips after Telegram init: /api/m/dashboard (BlockList.tsx) AND /api/m/activity/streak (DashboardClient.tsx). The streak route calls getWorkedDates() which fires 8 SEQUENTIAL Supabase queries (lib/activity/worked.ts, one per WORK_TABLES entry). One dashboard open = ~10+ serial DB queries + 2 HTTP hops, all after a blank paint. (3) Bundle/images — 57 of 72 files in /m are 'use client'. world-atlas/countries-110m.json (~100KB) is STATICALLY imported at top of onboarding/steps/GlobeSelect.tsx, landing in the onboarding chunk though Globe is dynamic(). next/image used ZERO times — every avatar is a raw hitting Supabase public Storage with no resize/cache (Avatar.tsx, tracking LeaderboardCard, panel/(dash)/students/studentBits.tsx, community PostCard). No images config in next.config.ts. Highest-leverage single change: one additive migration (parametrized *_filtered RPCs + covering indexes) speeds all three hot endpoints with no UI change.
spec.apiRoutes:
CHANGES (no new public routes; auth unchanged — all stay service-role/cookie-gated):

1. api/m/dashboard/route.ts (POST, Telegram initData) — merge the streak payload INTO the dashboard response so first paint needs ONE round-trip. loadDashboard.ts already runs a Promise.all; add student_daily_activity + getWorkedDates into the SAME server call and return `activity` alongside DashboardData. Removes the 2nd client fetch.

2. lib/activity/worked.ts getWorkedDates() — replace the 8 SEQUENTIAL awaited per-table queries with a single Promise.all over WORK_TABLES (parallel), or a worked_dates(p_user_id,p_since) RPC that UNION-ALLs created_at across the 8 tables server-side and returns DISTINCT bali-dates. 8 serial hops to 1.

3. api/m/tracking/route.ts (POST) — swap rpc('closed_dates_all') to rpc('closed_dates_filtered',{p_user_ids:ids}) and rpc('passed_blocks_all') to rpc('passed_blocks_filtered',{p_user_ids:ids}). ids already computed at line 90. Also fix `if (!ids.includes(row.user_id))` (O(n^2)) to a Set built once.

4. api/panel/students/route.ts + api/panel/stats/stats-data.ts — if option C adopted, read student_progress_summary instead of the two full-scan RPCs. Otherwise leave RPCs; they benefit from the new indexes.

5. app/panel/(dash)/layout.tsx + app/panel/(dash)/page.tsx — both independently call getPanelSession() and countPendingRequests(); page also re-runs resolveIsOwner. Wrap getPanelSession/resolveIsOwner/countPendingRequests in React cache() to dedupe within one request.

6. Add Cache-Control: private, max-age=30 header to api/m/tracking response (leaderboard tolerates 30s staleness) to absorb pull-to-refresh spam. revalidate not applicable (all per-user dynamic).
spec.rls: No RLS relaxation. All hot reads already go through createServiceSupabase() (service-role, bypasses RLS) server-side — correct and unchanged. New RPCs closed_dates_filtered/passed_blocks_filtered keep the existing model: SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path='', REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC, GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role only (mirrors the GRANT pattern in 20260618200000_v3_passed_blocks_helper.sql). If student_progress_summary (option C) is added: ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + single policy USING(false) so only service-role reads it — no client touches it. No anon/authenticated grants. Verify with mcp__supabase__get_advisors after applying.

Touch: Avatar.tsx, tracking/LeaderboardCard.tsx, panel/(dash)/students/studentBits.tsx, community/PostCard.tsx. Avatars are small thumbnails — serving full-res Storage originals is pure waste; even just width/height + lazy-loading off-screen leaderboard rows is a free win. Keeps ~100KB topojson out of the onboarding entry chunk until the globe mounts. No new design system, no shadcn additions. Russian strings unchanged.
implementationOrder: 1. MIGRATION FIRST (S): write supabase/migrations/20260622000000_v3_perf_indexes_and_summary.sql with section A (composite indexes) + section B (closed_dates_filtered + passed_blocks_filtered RPCs). Apply via supabase db push. Run mcp__supabase__get_advisors. Regenerate types + tsc --noEmit. ZERO app-code risk — indexes and additive functions only. Speeds all 3 hot endpoints.
