spec.risks:
EDGE CASES / RISKS:
- closed_dates_filtered must copy the 4-src logic and passed_blocks the 5-src logic EXACTLY (closed_dates_all uses HAVING count(DISTINCT src)=4 with a different recitations branch than passed_blocks_all which uses =5 incl. trainer). Do NOT unify them silently — that changes which days/blocks count as closed (a domain rule; flag to Михаил per CLAUDE.md). Just add the user-id filter.
- Day model is UTC-based today (todayUTC in block-status, (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date in RPCs) while streak/worked use Bali. This audit does NOT change the day boundary — separate correctness ticket (cities.timezone is unused). Keep UTC in the RPCs to avoid behavior change.
- next/image with Supabase Storage needs remotePatterns or images 404. CSP img-src already allows https: (next.config.ts line 32); /_next/image is same-origin so not CSP-blocked.
- Merging streak into dashboard changes the /api/m/dashboard response shape — bump the client consumer in the SAME PR; keep the old /api/m/activity/streak route (profile page uses it).
- React cache() only dedupes within a single request — correct here (layout+page run in one request); no cross-request leakage of session.
- New indexes add negligible write cost (daily tables are append-mostly, low write rate).
- Materialized summary (option C) risks staleness/refresh bugs — why it's last and optional.
- Regenerate types after migration or Vercel build silently runs old code (memory feedback_regen_types_after_migration).

critique.verdict: APPROVE WITH CHANGES. The performance audit is well-grounded — most code references (loadDashboardData, BlockList/DashboardClient split, worked.ts 8 serial queries, ProfileActivity reusing the streak route, panel layout+page double getPanelSession/countPendingRequests, next/image used zero times, CSP img-src 'self' covering /_next/image) are accurate against the real code, and the migration is correctly scoped as additive-only with no data loss. However, the spec contains one provably-WRONG domain claim about the RPC bodies, several redundant/duplicate indexes, a mis-described visibility property of the tracking route, and a GRANT pattern that no longer matches the live functions. Fix the corrections below before implementing; none of them break RLS, but the wrong RPC-difference claim could corrupt the discipleship gate if copied literally.
corrections: CRITICAL — the spec's 'risks' claim about the two RPCs is FACTUALLY WRONG and would mislead a literal implementer. It says closed_dates_all uses 'HAVING count(DISTINCT src)=4' while passed_blocks_all 'uses =5 incl. trainer'. That describes OLD overwritten definitions. Both functions are redefined 5+ times (20260618180000, 20260620100000, 20260620130000, 20260620140000, 20260620150000, and finally 20260620160000_v3_mestopisaniya_daily.sql — the LAST writer wins). In the FINAL live state BOTH use 4 sources (cross, prayer, recv=audio, loc=video_note-locations) with HAVING count(DISTINCT src)=4 and NEITHER includes the trainer table; passed_blocks_all in the final def also DROPPED the quiz_passed_at and friday_practice EXISTS checks. closed_dates_filtered/passed_blocks_filtered must be copied from 20260620160000 (the real current bodies), NOT from the 5-src/trainer versions the spec describes. Copying the trainer/=5 logic would silently re-add a dropped gate requirement — a domain-rule regression to flag to Михаил per CLAUDE.md.

The spec references only TWO migration files (20260620160000 + 20260618200000) as the source of truth for the RPCs, but 20260618200000 is itself an EARLIER, fully-overwritten definition (5-src + trainer + quiz + friday). Using it as the copy source is a trap. Section-by-section: there are 12 migrations touching closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all/user_closed_days/is_block_unlocked. The implementer MUST diff the final state (20260620160000) and ignore all earlier bodies.

Index idx_cross_ubd ON student_block_daily_cross(user_id, block_id, submitted_date) is a DUPLICATE — 20260510140200 already creates idx ON student_block_daily_cross(user_id, block_id, submitted_date DESC) AND the table has UNIQUE(user_id, block_id, submitted_date). Both already cover the GROUP BY. Drop this index from the migration (harmless with IF NOT EXISTS, but pure noise).

Indexes idx_recit_audio / idx_recit_video are largely redundant: 20260510140100 already has idx_sbr_user_block_medium ON student_block_recitations(user_id, block_id, medium, created_at DESC), which serves the medium='audio'/'video_note' grouped scans. The proposed partials add little. Similarly idx_sla_video_passed overlaps idx_sla_passed ON student_location_attempts(user_id, location_id) WHERE passed (already exists). Verify with EXPLAIN before adding — the real bottleneck is the post-JOIN GROUP BY on (user_id, l.block_id, date), which none of these single-table indexes accelerate.

Index idx_sda_user_opened ON student_daily_activity(user_id, activity_date) WHERE opened is redundant with the table's PRIMARY KEY (user_id, activity_date) for point/range lookups; the partial only helps opened-filtered aggregate scans. Keep only if EXPLAIN shows benefit. Note the existing idx_sda_date ON (activity_date) already exists too.

VISIBILITY MISREPRESENTATION (not an RLS hole, but a false framing): the spec says tracking 'scan is bounded to visible students' and calls ids 'видимых участников'. The actual route api/m/tracking/route.ts (lines 73-90) selects ALL students globally: .eq('role','student').eq('hidden_from_tracking', false) with NO is_visible_to / curator scoping. So ids = every student on the platform; closed_dates_filtered(ids) therefore scans the SAME rows as closed_dates_all — the advertised 'bounded scan' perf win is ~zero for the leaderboard. Also the proposed ids.includes()->Set optimization targets a check that is currently a no-op (every row's user is in ids). The genuine bounded-scan benefit only applies to the per-student MiniApp path, not the global leaderboard. Reword the spec to not claim visibility-safety, and consider that global-leaderboard visibility may itself be a separate domain question for Михаил.

GRANT pattern mismatch: the spec tells implementers to mirror 'REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role' from 20260618200000. But the FINAL definitions of closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all in 20260620160000 have NO REVOKE/GRANT at all (they rely on default PUBLIC EXECUTE). So the new *_filtered RPCs would be MORE locked-down than the originals they clone. That is arguably correct (service-role-only is safer) but it is an inconsistency to decide deliberately — and since these are SECURITY DEFINER, leaving default PUBLIC execute on a function that takes arbitrary p_user_ids[] and returns other users' closed-day data is a mild exposure if any authenticated client could call it. Recommend: explicitly REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC + GRANT EXECUTE TO service_role on the NEW functions (as the spec says) AND flag that the existing closed_dates_all/passed_blocks_all currently lack that lockdown.

worked.ts RPC proposal is under-specified: getWorkedDates reads created_at from EIGHT tables (student_quiz_attempts, student_location_attempts, student_block_recitations, student_block_daily_cross, student_block_daily_prayer, student_block_emotions, student_block_friday_practice, video_watch_progress). The spec's section-B index list covers none of student_block_emotions / student_block_friday_practice / video_watch_progress / student_quiz_attempts. A worked_dates(p_user_id,p_since) RPC must UNION all 8 and would benefit from a (user_id, created_at) index on each of those four uncovered tables — add them or the RPC still scans. Simpler/safer first step: just parallelize the existing 8 awaits with Promise.all (the spec's stated fallback) — zero schema risk.

React cache() over-claim (minor): the spec says to wrap getPanelSession + resolveIsOwner + countPendingRequests. Verified: layout.tsx and page.tsx BOTH call getPanelSession() and countPendingRequests() — caching those two genuinely dedupes. But resolveIsOwner (in the untracked apps/web/src/lib/admin/owner.ts) is called ONLY by page.tsx (not layout.tsx), so cache() gives it no dedup benefit within one render. Harmless, but correct the rationale. Also note resolveIsOwner's own docstring says it is intentionally NOT cached because is_protected is a privileged flag — using React cache() (per-request only, not cross-request) is consistent with that, but call it out so the intent isn't accidentally violated.

Timezone: the spec is CORRECT to keep the RPCs UTC-based and explicitly NOT change the day boundary (cities.timezone unused; block-status uses todayUTC; RPCs use (created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date while streak/worked use Bali). This is the right call for a perf-only PR — do NOT let the avatar/index work touch the day model. Recommend the spec add an explicit assertion that the *_filtered RPCs reproduce the COALESCE(effective_date,(created_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')::date) expression verbatim, because effective_date (the accelerated test-mode virtual date) is easy to drop when hand-copying — omitting it would break test-mode acceleration.

types regeneration target path: the spec says regenerate packages/supabase/src/types — verify this path exists in THIS repo (the memory note feedback_regen_types_after_migration is referenced correctly). Adding indexes alone does not change generated types; only the two new RPC function signatures do, so tsc --noEmit + regen is needed specifically because tracking/route.ts currently casts rpc() through (supabase as any) — the new typed signatures should remove those casts or at least not regress them.
